title: Upload XPRIZE dendogram SVG to PDS as observations.dendogram record
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/upload-xprize-dendogram.js (create)

## What to do
Create a one-off migration script that downloads the XPRIZE dendogram SVG from S3 and creates an app.gainforest.organization.observations.dendogram record on the PDS.

1. Download the SVG from: https://gainforest-transparency-dashboard.s3.amazonaws.com/dendogram/xprize-rainforest-finals_dendogram.svg
2. Login to the XPRIZE organization's PDS account. There are 3 XPRIZE handles:
   - xprize-rainforest.climateai.org (did:plc:y6oovsehm62hkctcwcovbss5) — this is the main one
   - xprize-2024.climateai.org (did:plc:je4bmysje3yvaxf2wwo643z3)
   - xprize-rainfor-21p.climateai.org (did:plc:axibb3hd3od3635jhihwmohv)
   Use the main xprize-rainforest handle. If credentials are missing, try the others.
3. Upload the SVG as a PDS blob via agent.uploadBlob(svgBuffer, { encoding: 'image/svg+xml' })
4. Create the dendogram record with key 'self' (literal:self):
   - dendogram: { $type: 'org.hypercerts.defs#smallBlob', ref: blobRef, mimeType: 'image/svg+xml', size: svgBuffer.length }
   - createdAt: new Date().toISOString()
   - name: 'Flora Phylogenetic Tree - XPRIZE Rainforest Finals'
   - treeType: 'phylogenetic'
   - taxonGroups: ['flora']
   - dataSource: 'XPRIZE Rainforest Competition'
5. Write via agent.com.atproto.repo.putRecord({ repo: did, collection: 'app.gainforest.organization.observations.dendogram', rkey: 'self', record })

### CLI flags:
--service, --credentials, --dry-run, --handle (default: xprize-rainforest.climateai.org)

## Don't
- Create dendogram records for any other organization (only XPRIZE has one)
- Modify any frontend files
- Create a frontend component for dendograms (that's a separate concern)
acceptance_criteria:
1. scripts/migrations/upload-xprize-dendogram.js exists and runs without error
2. The SVG is downloaded from S3 successfully
3. The SVG is uploaded as a PDS blob
4. An observations.dendogram record is created with rkey 'self' on the XPRIZE org
5. The record includes name, treeType, taxonGroups, and dataSource fields
6. Dry-run mode shows what would be created without writing
7. bun run lint passes

title: Epic: Fix trees not showing for Oceanus Conservation and other truncated-handle orgs
description:
## Problem
Trees are not showing when clicking on 'Oceanus Conservation' (did:plc:6oxtzu7gxz7xcldvtwfh3bpt, handle: oceanus-conservati.climateai.org) on the globe.

## Root Cause Analysis
Two compounding issues prevent trees from displaying:

### Issue 1: Wrong blob CID on PDS site records (PRIMARY)
During tree data migration, all 5 Oceanus Conservation site records were updated with blob CID bafkreiclcaikdxi7nrls45gllljlzcjmgzvmv2pnh43xpcuhqbhmacwqvu (187 bytes, empty features array). The correct 4.1MB blob with actual tree data (bafkreibghofvcze46eh6p3x3oryq5iel4g4z4nnzhdooumub4milzs2d3y) was uploaded but never referenced by any site record (last-write-wins during migration overwrote correct refs with empty ones). The correct blob is likely garbage-collected.

### Issue 2: S3 fallback uses truncated slug (SECONDARY)
Even if the PDS blob fetch failed, the S3 fallback would construct the wrong URL. fetchOrganizationSlug() derives slug from handle.split('.')[0] = 'oceanus-conservati' (truncated by PDS to 18 chars), but the S3 file is named 'oceanus-conservation-all-tree-plantings.geojson'. Migration scripts have a SLUG_OVERRIDES map for this, but the frontend does not.

### Issue 3: No empty-features guard (DEFENSIVE)
fetchMeasuredTreesShapefile() in store/utils.ts treats any 200 OK response as success, even if features array is empty. It should fall through to S3 fallback when blob returns empty features.

## Success Criteria
- Trees display correctly when clicking Oceanus Conservation on the globe
- S3 fallback works for all orgs with truncated handles (18+ char names)
- Empty blob responses trigger fallback instead of silently showing nothing

## Scope
- Frontend code fix (slug overrides + empty-features guard)
- PDS data fix (re-upload correct tree blob for Oceanus Conservation)
- Affects: src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/store/
close_reason: 21e92bd all children (39.1, 39.2, 39.3) completed

title: Write targeted migration script to re-upload correct tree blob for Oceanus Conservation
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/fix-oceanus-tree-blob.js (create)

## Context
During the original tree migration, all 5 Oceanus Conservation site records ended up referencing blob CID bafkreiclcaikdxi7nrls45gllljlzcjmgzvmv2pnh43xpcuhqbhmacwqvu (187 bytes, empty features array). The correct tree data exists on S3 at:
https://gainforest-transparency-dashboard.s3.amazonaws.com/shapefiles/oceanus-conservation-all-tree-plantings.geojson (~4.1MB with actual tree features).

The correct blob was originally uploaded as bafkreibghofvcze46eh6p3x3oryq5iel4g4z4nnzhdooumub4milzs2d3y but is likely garbage-collected since no records reference it.

## What to do
Create a standalone Node.js script that:

1. Downloads the correct tree GeoJSON from S3:
   URL: https://gainforest-transparency-dashboard.s3.amazonaws.com/shapefiles/oceanus-conservation-all-tree-plantings.geojson

2. Validates it has non-empty features array and is valid JSON

3. Logs in to PDS as Oceanus Conservation (using credentials from tmp/pds-credentials.json, key: 'oceanus-conservati.climateai.org')

4. Uploads the GeoJSON as a blob via agent.com.atproto.repo.uploadBlob()

5. Lists all site records for did:plc:6oxtzu7gxz7xcldvtwfh3bpt in collection app.gainforest.organization.site

6. For each site record, updates it with putRecord to set trees field to:
   { $type: 'app.gainforest.common.defs#smallBlob', blob: <uploaded blob ref> }
   preserving all other fields (spread existing record value)

7. Verifies each updated site by re-fetching and checking the trees blob CID matches

Script should:
- Accept --service URL flag (default: https://climateai.org)
- Accept --dry-run flag
- Log each step clearly
- Save results to tmp/fix-oceanus-tree-results.json

Follow the same patterns as scripts/migrations/upload-and-update-site-trees-atomic.js (use @atproto/api AtpAgent, same login/upload/putRecord pattern).

## Organization details
- DID: did:plc:6oxtzu7gxz7xcldvtwfh3bpt
- Handle: oceanus-conservati.climateai.org
- 5 sites: lobo, salay, aringay, aras-asan, Cagwait - Poblacion
- Site rkeys: 3m2d63roz2c26, 3m2d63rhiss26, 3m2d63r7zkk26, 3m2d63qyegs26, 3m2d63qbymk26

## Don't
- Don't modify any existing migration scripts
- Don't modify any frontend code
- Don't touch any other organization's data
- Don't use hardcoded passwords — read from tmp/pds-credentials.json
- Don't skip the dry-run flag support
acceptance_criteria:
1. Script downloads oceanus-conservation-all-tree-plantings.geojson from S3 and validates it has features.length > 0
2. Script uploads the blob and updates all 5 site records with the new blob ref
3. --dry-run flag prevents any PDS writes
4. Results are saved to tmp/fix-oceanus-tree-results.json
5. After running (non-dry-run), fetching https://climateai.org/xrpc/com.atproto.sync.getBlob?did=did:plc:6oxtzu7gxz7xcldvtwfh3bpt&cid=<new-cid> returns GeoJSON with non-empty features
6. bun run lint passes (script uses eslint-disable comments for require() like other migration scripts)

title: Fix: audio.json metadata fields missing maxGraphemes and integer minimum constraints (from green_globe-1.3)
description: Review of green_globe-1.3 found multiple missing constraints in organization/recordings/audio.json. (1) String fields missing maxGraphemes: metadata.codec (no maxGraphemes, no format), metadata.duration (no maxGraphemes, no format), metadata.coordinates (no maxGraphemes, no format — this is a pre-existing field but the task touched the file and should have added it), main.name (no maxGraphemes, no format). (2) Integer fields missing minimum constraints: metadata.channels has no minimum (should be minimum: 1), metadata.sampleRate has no minimum (should be minimum: 1 or minimum: 0). These are pre-existing fields that the task did not fix, but AC9 and AC10 require 'all string fields have maxGraphemes constraints' and 'all integer fields have appropriate minimum/maximum'. The new fields added by the task (bitDepth, fileSizeBytes, minFrequencyHz, maxFrequencyHz) do have correct constraints.

title: Epic: Migrate images from S3/external sources to ATProto PDS
description:
## Goal
Migrate tree measurement photos and species occurrence images from AWS S3 (and other external sources) to the ATProto PDS at climateai.org, using dwc.occurrence as the unified record type for all biodiversity data.

## Context
- 27,821 tree photos on S3 (107.5 GB raw), ~15,147 referenced in GeoJSON files across 91 org GeoJSON files
- 3,477 species/Restor files on S3 (686 MB) with species image URLs in dwc.occurrence records
- Tree photos currently embedded as URL strings inside GeoJSON blobs on site records (undeclared 'trees' field)
- Species images stored as speciesImageUrl/thumbnailUrl strings on dwc.occurrence, imageEvidence blob field exists but is never populated
- Airtable dependency already removed (prior epic green_globe-16)

## Key Decisions (locked in)
1. Use dwc.occurrence for measured trees (basisOfRecord: HumanObservation) — NOT a new measuredTree lexicon
2. Add trunkEvidence, leafEvidence, barkEvidence fields to dwc.occurrence lexicon (app.gainforest.common.defs#image)
3. Discriminate measured trees via basisOfRecord (HumanObservation vs MachineObservation) + dynamicProperties.dataType
4. Compress images to 800px wide, WebP quality 80 (~340KB avg) for PDS; keep full-res on S3
5. Populate imageEvidence blob on species occurrences, then remove speciesImageUrl/thumbnailUrl string fields
6. Each tree = 1 dwc.occurrence + N dwc.measurement records for DBH/height
7. Migration scripts must be idempotent and resumable, streaming one-at-a-time (download → compress → upload → delete local)
8. Deprecate measuredTreesCluster lexicon and undeclared trees field on site records
9. KoboToolbox work deferred until tree photo architecture is settled
10. Animals have zero images (predictions only) — no image migration needed for fauna

## Scope
- Lexicon changes to dwc.occurrence (add 3 image fields)
- Migration script for measured tree photos (S3 → PDS blobs + new dwc.occurrence/measurement records)
- Migration script for species occurrence images (download from source URLs → compress → upload as imageEvidence blobs)
- Frontend updates to read measured trees from dwc.occurrence instead of GeoJSON blobs
- Frontend updates to read imageEvidence blobs instead of speciesImageUrl strings
- Cleanup: deprecate old data paths, fix existing bugs (cover image URL case, next.config wildcard)

## Out of Scope
- KoboToolbox photo migration (deferred)
- Google Drive photos (Oceanus Conservation — needs separate access investigation)
- Animal species images (none exist)
- Map tile imagery (Mapbox, Planet, ESA Terrascope — third-party services)
- Layer thumbnails (separate effort)
- Audio recordings/spectrograms (separate effort)

## Success Criteria
- All referenced tree photos (~6,449 from S3) uploaded as compressed blobs on dwc.occurrence records
- All species occurrence images uploaded as imageEvidence blobs
- Frontend displays tree photos and species images from PDS blobs
- No S3 image URLs remain in active frontend code paths (S3 kept as fallback only during transition)
- speciesImageUrl/thumbnailUrl fields deprecated on occurrence records
close_reason: cf11b77 superseded by epic 46 (Audubon Core adoption)

title: Add trunkEvidence, leafEvidence, barkEvidence fields to dwc.occurrence lexicon
description:
## Files
- lexicons/app/gainforest/dwc/occurrence.json (modify)

## What to do
Add three new optional fields to the dwc.occurrence record properties, placed after the existing imageEvidence field (line 429):

```json
"trunkEvidence": {
  "type": "ref",
  "ref": "app.gainforest.common.defs#image",
  "description": "Image of the trunk or whole organism. Used for measured tree records (basisOfRecord: HumanObservation). Compressed to 800px wide WebP on PDS; full-res kept on external storage."
},
"leafEvidence": {
  "type": "ref",
  "ref": "app.gainforest.common.defs#image",
  "description": "Image of leaves or foliage. Used for measured tree records (basisOfRecord: HumanObservation). Compressed to 800px wide WebP on PDS; full-res kept on external storage."
},
"barkEvidence": {
  "type": "ref",
  "ref": "app.gainforest.common.defs#image",
  "description": "Image of bark. Used for measured tree records (basisOfRecord: HumanObservation). Compressed to 800px wide WebP on PDS; full-res kept on external storage."
}
```

All three use `app.gainforest.common.defs#image` (20MB cap, accepts JPEG/PNG/WebP/HEIC/TIFF/GIF/BMP/SVG). This is the same type used by the existing `imageEvidence` field.

None of the three fields are required — they are optional and only populated for measured tree occurrences.

## Don't
- Don't change any existing fields
- Don't add these to the required array
- Don't modify any other lexicon files
- Don't run codegen (that's a separate task)
acceptance_criteria:
1. occurrence.json parses as valid JSON
2. The three new fields exist under defs.main.record.properties
3. Each field has type 'ref' with ref 'app.gainforest.common.defs#image'
4. No existing fields were modified
5. The required array is unchanged (still only basisOfRecord, scientificName, eventDate, createdAt)
6. `bun run lint` passes
close_reason: 3630046 Add trunkEvidence, leafEvidence, barkEvidence fields to dwc.occurrence lexicon

title: Redo 40.4: Write species occurrence image migration script with atomic blob referencing
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-occurrence-images-to-blobs.js (create)

## Context
The previous attempt at this task (green_globe-40.4) was reported as complete but the code file was never committed to git. This task must be redone from scratch.

CRITICAL: The ATProto blob spec requires that uploaded blobs be referenced by a record before the PDS garbage collects them. The pattern MUST be: upload blob → immediately putRecord to reference it. See `atproto-docs/specs/blobs.md` lines 27-37.

## What to do
Create an idempotent, resumable migration script that populates the `imageEvidence` blob field on existing `dwc.occurrence` records that currently only have `speciesImageUrl`/`thumbnailUrl` string fields.

### For each organization DID
1. List all dwc.occurrence records via `com.atproto.repo.listRecords({ collection: 'app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence' })`
2. Filter to records where:
   - `basisOfRecord === 'MachineObservation'` (species predictions, not measured trees)
   - `speciesImageUrl` or `thumbnailUrl` is a non-empty string URL
   - `imageEvidence` is NOT already populated (skip already-migrated records)

### For each matching record — ATOMIC BLOB REFERENCING
1. Download the image from the `speciesImageUrl` (or `thumbnailUrl` fallback)
2. Compress using sharp: resize to 800px wide (withoutEnlargement: true), convert to WebP quality 80
3. Upload via `agent.uploadBlob(buffer, { encoding: 'image/webp' })`
4. **IMMEDIATELY** update the record via `putRecord` to add `imageEvidence: { $type: 'app.gainforest.common.defs#image', file: blobRef }`
   - The putRecord call MUST happen immediately after uploadBlob — no other operations in between
   - This ensures the blob is referenced before the PDS can garbage collect it
   - Preserve ALL existing fields on the record (spread the existing record value, then add imageEvidence)
5. Keep `speciesImageUrl` and `thumbnailUrl` on the record for now (removal is a separate task)

### Preserving existing record fields
When calling putRecord, you MUST preserve all existing fields:
```javascript
const existingRecord = await agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord({
  repo: did,
  collection: DWC_OCCURRENCE_COLLECTION,
  rkey: rkey
})
const updatedRecord = {
  ...existingRecord.data.value,
  imageEvidence: { $type: 'app.gainforest.common.defs#image', file: blobRef }
}
await agent.com.atproto.repo.putRecord({
  repo: did,
  collection: DWC_OCCURRENCE_COLLECTION,
  rkey: rkey,
  record: updatedRecord
})
```

### Resumability
- Progress file at `scripts/migrations/data/occurrence-images-progress.json`
- Track: `{ orgDid: { completedRkeys: string[] } }`
- On restart, skip completed rkeys

### Error handling
- Log failures to `scripts/migrations/data/occurrence-images-errors.json`
- Some image URLs may be dead (404) — log and skip, don't fail
- Retry downloads up to 3 times with exponential backoff
- Continue on individual record failure

### Auth
Same pattern as `migrate-members-to-pds.js`: reads PDS credentials from `tmp/pds-credentials.json`, uses `--service`, `--handle`, `--limit`, `--dry-run` flags.

### Reference
- Follow the same CLI arg pattern as `migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js`
- Follow the atomic blob pattern from `upload-and-update-site-cover-images-atomic.js`

## Don't
- Don't create new occurrence records (only update existing ones)
- Don't remove speciesImageUrl/thumbnailUrl fields yet (backward compat during transition)
- Don't process records that already have imageEvidence populated
- Don't modify any frontend code or lexicon files
- Don't leave uploaded blobs unreferenced — every uploadBlob MUST be followed immediately by putRecord
acceptance_criteria:
1. Script file exists at scripts/migrations/migrate-occurrence-images-to-blobs.js
2. Script runs without crashing on at least one org's occurrence data (--dry-run mode)
3. Each uploadBlob call is IMMEDIATELY followed by a putRecord that references the blob
4. Records with speciesImageUrl now also have imageEvidence blob ref populated
5. Compressed images are 800px wide WebP at quality 80
6. speciesImageUrl and thumbnailUrl fields are preserved (not removed)
7. Records that already had imageEvidence are skipped (idempotent)
8. Progress file enables resumability
9. Dead URLs (404) are logged and skipped gracefully
10. Running twice produces identical PDS state
11. All existing record fields are preserved when updating (no field loss)
12. `bun run lint` passes
close_reason: bc03814 Write species occurrence image migration script with atomic blob referencing

title: Write migration script: measured tree photos from S3 to PDS as dwc.occurrence records
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js (create)

## What to do
Create an idempotent, resumable migration script that:

### Phase 1: For each organization with tree GeoJSON data
1. List all site records for the org via `com.atproto.repo.listRecords({ collection: 'app.gainforest.organization.site' })`
2. For each site with a `trees` blob ref, fetch the GeoJSON blob via `com.atproto.sync.getBlob`
3. If no blob ref, fall back to S3: `https://gainforest-transparency-dashboard.s3.amazonaws.com/shapefiles/{slug}-all-tree-plantings.geojson`

### Phase 2: For each tree feature in the GeoJSON
1. Check if a dwc.occurrence already exists for this tree (idempotency — use occurrenceID as dedup key, constructed from org DID + tree coordinates + species)
2. If not, create a `dwc.occurrence` record with:
   - `basisOfRecord: 'HumanObservation'`
   - `kingdom: 'Plantae'`
   - `scientificName`: from feature properties (`species`, `Plant_Name`, or `scientificName`)
   - `eventDate`: from `dateMeasured` property (ISO 8601) or current date as fallback
   - `decimalLatitude`, `decimalLongitude`: from feature geometry coordinates
   - `siteRef`: AT-URI of the site record
   - `dynamicProperties`: JSON string with `{"source":"field","dataType":"measuredTree","originalAwsUrl":"...","originalKoboUrl":"..."}`
   - `associatedMedia`: pipe-delimited original S3/Kobo URLs (for full-res reference)
   - `createdAt`: current ISO datetime

### Phase 3: Upload compressed photos as blobs
For each photo URL on the tree feature (awsUrl, leafAwsUrl, barkAwsUrl — falling back to koboUrl variants):
1. Download the image from S3/Kobo
2. Compress using sharp: resize to 800px wide (withoutEnlargement: true), convert to WebP quality 80
3. Upload via `agent.uploadBlob(buffer, { encoding: 'image/webp' })`
4. Set the blob ref on the occurrence record:
   - awsUrl/koboUrl → `trunkEvidence: { $type: 'app.gainforest.common.defs#image', file: blobRef }`
   - leafAwsUrl/leafKoboUrl → `leafEvidence: { ... }`
   - barkAwsUrl/barkKoboUrl → `barkEvidence: { ... }`
5. Delete the local temp file immediately after upload

### Phase 4: Create dwc.measurement records
For each tree with DBH or height data:
1. Create `dwc.measurement` record with `measurementType: 'DBH'`, `measurementValue`, `measurementUnit: 'cm'`, linked via `occurrenceRef`
2. Create `dwc.measurement` record with `measurementType: 'height'`, `measurementValue`, `measurementUnit: 'm'`, linked via `occurrenceRef`

### Resumability
- Maintain a progress file at `scripts/migrations/data/measured-trees-progress.json` tracking: `{ orgDid: { siteRkey: { lastProcessedFeatureIndex: N, completedFeatures: [...] } } }`
- On restart, skip already-completed features
- Use `putRecord` (not `createRecord`) with a deterministic rkey derived from the tree's unique identifier to ensure idempotency

### Error handling
- Log failures to `scripts/migrations/data/measured-trees-errors.json`
- Continue processing other trees on individual failure
- Retry failed image downloads up to 3 times with exponential backoff

### Special cases
- Oceanus Conservation uses `FCD-tree_records-tree_photo` (Google Drive URLs) — log these as skipped with reason 'google-drive-url', do not attempt download
- Ayyoweca Uganda uses `media_sources.images[]` format — handle via the same converter logic in `ayyoweca-uganda.ts`
- Some GeoJSON files have 51-byte empty files on S3 — skip these gracefully
- Koko DAO has the same 189 trees duplicated across 9 site GeoJSONs — the deterministic rkey ensures dedup

### Dependencies
- Requires: `@atproto/api`, `sharp`, `csv-parse` (already in package.json)
- Auth: uses PDS_ENDPOINT and PDS credentials from .env (same pattern as existing migration scripts like `migrate-members-to-pds.js`)

## Don't
- Don't modify any frontend code
- Don't modify any lexicon files
- Don't delete or modify existing PDS records (only create new ones)
- Don't download all images at once — stream one at a time to stay within 12GB disk
- Don't process Google Drive URLs (Oceanus) — just log them as skipped
acceptance_criteria:
1. Script runs without crashing on at least one org's tree data
2. Creates dwc.occurrence records with basisOfRecord 'HumanObservation' and dynamicProperties containing dataType 'measuredTree'
3. Compressed images are 800px wide WebP at quality 80
4. Each occurrence has trunkEvidence/leafEvidence/barkEvidence blob refs where photos exist
5. dwc.measurement records are created for DBH and height where data exists
6. Progress file is written and script can resume from where it left off
7. Errors are logged to error file, not swallowed silently
8. No more than one image file exists on disk at any time during execution
9. Google Drive URLs (Oceanus) are logged as skipped, not attempted
10. Running the script twice produces no duplicate records (idempotent via deterministic rkeys)
close_reason: bc03814 Write migration script: measured tree photos from S3 to PDS as dwc.occurrence records

title: Update frontend: read measured trees from dwc.occurrence instead of GeoJSON blobs
description:
## Files
- src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_hooks/use-organization-measured-trees.ts (create)
- src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/store/index.ts (modify)
- src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/store/utils.ts (modify)
- src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/store/types.ts (modify)
- src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/Map/sources-and-layers/measured-trees.ts (modify)
- src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/HoveredTreeOverlay/index.tsx (modify)
- src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/HoveredTreeOverlay/store.ts (modify)

## What to do

### 1. Create new hook: use-organization-measured-trees.ts
Create a React Query hook that fetches measured tree data from dwc.occurrence records:
- Query `app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence` collection for the org DID
- Filter to `basisOfRecord === 'HumanObservation'` records where `dynamicProperties` contains `dataType: 'measuredTree'`
- For each occurrence, extract:
  - `scientificName`, `vernacularName` → species/common name
  - `decimalLatitude`, `decimalLongitude` → coordinates
  - `trunkEvidence`, `leafEvidence`, `barkEvidence` → construct PDS blob URLs using `getBlobUrl()` from `@/lib/atproto/sdk-utils` or manual `${PDS_ENDPOINT}/xrpc/com.atproto.sync.getBlob?did=${did}&cid=${cid}` pattern
  - `associatedMedia` → original S3 URLs (for full-res fallback)
- Also fetch linked `dwc.measurement` records for DBH and height
- Return data as a GeoJSON FeatureCollection compatible with the existing `MeasuredTreesGeoJSON` type (so the Mapbox source can consume it directly)

### 2. Update store/types.ts
Add a new type `MeasuredTreeOccurrence` that maps the dwc.occurrence fields to the existing `TreeFeatureProperties` shape. The key mapping:
- `trunkEvidence` blob URL → `awsUrl` (reuse existing field name for backward compat with getTreePhotos)
- `leafEvidence` blob URL → `leafAwsUrl`
- `barkEvidence` blob URL → `barkAwsUrl`
- `associatedMedia` original URLs → `koboUrl`, `leafKoboUrl`, `barkKoboUrl` (as fallbacks)

### 3. Update store/index.ts
In `activateSite()`:
- Add a new code path that tries the new hook's data first
- Fall back to the existing `fetchMeasuredTreesShapefile()` for orgs that haven't been migrated yet
- The fallback ensures zero downtime during the migration rollout

### 4. Update measured-trees.ts getTreePhotos()
- Add PDS blob URL detection: if a URL contains `com.atproto.sync.getBlob`, use it directly (no S3 fallback needed)
- Keep the existing S3/Kobo fallback chain for non-migrated trees
- Remove the hardcoded project ID exception (line 190) once that org is migrated

### 5. Update HoveredTreeOverlay
- No changes needed if the TreeFeatureProperties shape is preserved (the overlay reads `treePhotos[0]` which is just a URL string regardless of source)
- Verify that next/image can optimize PDS blob URLs (the wildcard `hostname: '*'` in next.config.ts covers this)

## Don't
- Don't remove the existing GeoJSON blob fetch path yet (keep as fallback)
- Don't modify any migration scripts
- Don't modify any lexicon files
- Don't break the existing measured trees display for non-migrated orgs
- Don't change the Mapbox source/layer setup (it consumes GeoJSON regardless of source)
acceptance_criteria:
1. Measured trees from migrated orgs display on the map from dwc.occurrence records
2. Tree photos load from PDS blob URLs (visible in network tab as climateai.org/xrpc/com.atproto.sync.getBlob requests)
3. HoveredTreeOverlay shows trunk photo, species name, DBH, and height from occurrence/measurement records
4. Non-migrated orgs still work via the existing GeoJSON blob fallback
5. `bun run lint` passes
6. `bun run build` passes
7. No TypeScript errors

## What to do

### 1. Update use-organization-occurrences.ts
In `RawOccurrenceValue` type (line 34), add:
```typescript
imageEvidence?: { $type?: string; file?: { ref?: unknown; mimeType?: string } };
```

In `normalizePlantRecord()` (line 147), update the image URL resolution:
1. First, try to extract a PDS blob URL from `imageEvidence.file.ref`:
   - Extract CID from the BlobRef (handle both `.$link` string and CID object formats — see `extractCid()` in `store/utils.ts` lines 53-74 for the pattern)
   - Construct URL: `${PDS_ENDPOINT}/xrpc/com.atproto.sync.getBlob?did=${did}&cid=${cid}`
   - Note: the `did` must be passed into the normalizer — currently it's not available. Add it as a parameter to `normalizePlantRecord` and `fetchAllOccurrenceRecords`.
2. Fall back to `speciesImageUrl` → `thumbnailUrl` (existing logic, lines 164-168)

### 2. Update Predictions/store/utils.ts
Apply the same changes to the duplicated occurrence-fetching code in this file:
- `fetchPlantsFromATProto()` (line 234)
- Same imageEvidence → blob URL resolution logic

### 3. Update Predictions/store/types.ts
No type changes needed — `BiodiversityPlant.awsUrl` and `imageUrl` remain string fields. The value just changes from an S3 URL to a PDS blob URL.

### Key implementation detail
The `PDS_ENDPOINT` is available from `@/config/atproto` (imported as `PDS_ENDPOINT`). Use lowercase `com.atproto.sync.getBlob` (NOT `com.atProto` — there's an existing case bug in ProjectOverlay/index.tsx that should not be replicated).

title: Fix 40.5: getTreePhotos placeholder guard is dead code — PDS trees always show awsUrl, never placeholder
Fix 40.5: fetchPlantsFromATProto includes HumanObservation measured trees in species predictions panel
description:
## Review of green_globe-40.5 found: measured tree records bleed into biodiversity predictions panel

`fetchPlantsFromATProto` in `src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/Biodiversity/Predictions/store/utils.ts` (lines 270-315) filters only by `kingdom === 'Plantae'`. It does NOT filter by `basisOfRecord`.

After green_globe-40.5 lands, migrated orgs will have `HumanObservation` dwc.occurrence records (measured trees) in their collection alongside `MachineObservation` species prediction records. Both have `kingdom: 'Plantae'`. `fetchPlantsFromATProto` will therefore include measured trees in the species predictions panel — showing individual field-measured trees as if they were species predictions, with trunk photo URLs as species images.

## Evidence
- `Predictions/store/utils.ts` line 293: `if (kingdom !== 'Plantae') continue;` — no basisOfRecord check
- `use-organization-measured-trees.ts` line 241: measured trees are `basisOfRecord === 'HumanObservation'`
- `Predictions/store/index.ts` line 74: calls `fetchPlantsFromATProto(did)` with no filter

## Fix
Add a `basisOfRecord !== 'HumanObservation'` guard inside the loop in `fetchPlantsFromATProto`:

```ts
// Skip measured trees — they are HumanObservation records, not species predictions
const basisOfRecord =
  typeof record.value.basisOfRecord === 'string'
    ? record.value.basisOfRecord
    : undefined;
if (basisOfRecord === 'HumanObservation') continue;
```

This mirrors the existing filter in `use-organization-occurrences.ts` which already has an `OccurrenceFilter` mechanism for this purpose.
close_reason: d3c1892 fix: exclude HumanObservation records from species predictions panel

title: Fix 40.5+40.6: extractCid triplicated across three files — extract to shared utility
description:
## Review of green_globe-40.5 and green_globe-40.6 found: extractCid duplicated three times

The `extractCid` helper function is now copy-pasted identically in three separate files:

1. `src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/store/utils.ts` (lines 53-74) — original
2. `src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_hooks/use-organization-measured-trees.ts` (lines 88-105) — added by 40.5
3. `src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_hooks/use-organization-occurrences.ts` (lines 152-169) — added by 40.6
4. `src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/Biodiversity/Predictions/store/utils.ts` (lines 147-164) — added by 40.6

All four implementations are byte-for-byte identical. The spec for 40.6 explicitly called out 'see extractCid() in store/utils.ts lines 53-74 for the pattern' — meaning the intent was to reuse, not copy.

## Risk
Any future bug fix or CID format change must be applied in four places. This has already caused one bug (the com.atProto case bug in 40.8 was a similar copy-paste divergence).

## Fix
Export `extractCid` from `store/utils.ts` (or move it to `@/lib/atproto/sdk-utils` if one exists) and import it in the three other files. Remove the three duplicate definitions.

title: Fix 40.5: fetchMeasuredTreeOccurrences fetches ALL occurrences then filters client-side — O(N) unnecessary data transfer
description:
## Review of green_globe-40.5 found: full occurrence collection fetched before filtering to measured trees

`fetchMeasuredTreeOccurrences` in `use-organization-measured-trees.ts` (lines 207-232) paginates through the entire `app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence` collection for an org, then filters client-side to `basisOfRecord === 'HumanObservation'` + `dynamicProperties.dataType === 'measuredTree'`.

For orgs with large species prediction datasets (e.g. 3,477 Restor records), this means downloading all species occurrence records over the network just to discard them. The `com.atproto.repo.listRecords` XRPC endpoint does not support server-side filtering, so this is unavoidable with the current ATProto API — but the parallel fetch of measurements (also full collection) compounds the problem.

## Evidence
`use-organization-measured-trees.ts` lines 207-232: no filter parameter passed to `listRecords`, all records fetched then filtered at lines 235-247.

## Context / Mitigation
This is a known ATProto limitation (no server-side filtering on listRecords). The correct long-term fix is to store measured trees in a separate collection (e.g. `app.gainforest.dwc.measuredTree`) or use a dedicated index record. Short-term, the fallback to `null` when no measured trees are found (line 249) means non-migrated orgs pay this cost unnecessarily on every site activation.

## Fix options (for Lead to decide)
1. Add a lightweight 'has measured trees' flag to the org record to skip the fetch entirely for non-migrated orgs
2. Accept the current behavior as a known limitation and document it
3. Move measured trees to a separate ATProto collection

File this as a performance issue for the Lead to triage — do not block 40.5 on it.

title: Fix 40.5: MeasuredTreeOccurrence type defined in types.ts but never used — dead type
description:
## Review of green_globe-40.5 found: MeasuredTreeOccurrence type is dead code

The spec for 40.5 asked for a `MeasuredTreeOccurrence` type to be added to `store/types.ts`. It was added (lines 169-174) but is never imported or used anywhere in the codebase. The hook (`use-organization-measured-trees.ts`) builds `NormalizedTreeFeature` objects directly without going through `MeasuredTreeOccurrence`.

The type also declares `_source` and `_occurrenceUri` fields that are never populated on any feature — so even if the type were used, those fields would always be undefined at runtime.

## Evidence
- `store/types.ts` lines 169-174: type defined
- `use-organization-measured-trees.ts`: imports `NormalizedTreeFeature` and `MeasuredTreesGeoJSON` from types.ts, but NOT `MeasuredTreeOccurrence`
- No other file imports `MeasuredTreeOccurrence`

## Fix
Either:
a) Remove `MeasuredTreeOccurrence` from types.ts (it adds no value if unused), OR
b) Use it as the intermediate type in the hook's mapping step and populate `_source` / `_occurrenceUri` for future debuggability

Option (a) is simpler. Option (b) is only worth doing if the `_occurrenceUri` field will be used by a future feature (e.g. deep-linking to the PDS record).

title: Epic: Adopt Audubon Core for biodiversity media on ATProto PDS
description:
## Goal
Replace the hardcoded evidence fields on dwc.occurrence (trunkEvidence, leafEvidence, barkEvidence, imageEvidence, audioEvidence, videoEvidence, spectrogramEvidence) with a standards-aligned Audubon Core multimedia record type. Each media item becomes its own record linked to an occurrence, using the TDWG ac:subjectPart controlled vocabulary.

## Context
- Epic green_globe-40 added trunkEvidence/leafEvidence/barkEvidence to dwc.occurrence, but this approach is rigid (only 3 photo types), non-standard, and caused blob GC issues
- Audubon Core (AC) is the official TDWG/GBIF standard for biodiversity media metadata — the companion to Darwin Core
- AC has a controlled vocabulary (ac:subjectPart) with terms for bark, leaf, entireOrganism, flower, fruit, seed, stem, etc.
- Migration scripts from green_globe-40 have NOT been run in production yet — no data to migrate
- Frontend tasks from green_globe-40 (40.5, 40.6) are done but read from fields that will be replaced

## Key Decisions
1. Create new lexicon: app.gainforest.ac.multimedia — one record per media item
2. Each AC record links to a dwc.occurrence via occurrenceRef (at-uri)
3. Use ac:subjectPart controlled values: entireOrganism, leaf, bark, flower, fruit, seed, stem
4. Remove trunkEvidence/leafEvidence/barkEvidence from dwc.occurrence (added in 40.1, never populated in production)
5. Keep imageEvidence on dwc.occurrence for now (backward compat for species predictions already in production) — migrate to AC in a follow-up
6. Migration scripts use naturally atomic pattern: uploadBlob → createRecord (one AC record per blob)
7. Compress images to 800px wide WebP q80 (~340KB avg) — same as green_globe-40
8. Frontend reads AC records via listRecords filtered by occurrenceRef

## Supersedes
- green_globe-40.1 (lexicon fields — will be reverted)
- green_globe-40.3/40.12 (tree migration script — will be rewritten)
- green_globe-40.4/40.13 (species image migration — will be rewritten)
- green_globe-40.5 (frontend measured trees — will be updated)
- green_globe-40.6 (frontend species images — will be updated)
- green_globe-40.7, 40.10, 40.11 (cancelled cleanup tasks)

## Success Criteria
- New app.gainforest.ac.multimedia lexicon exists with subjectPart, file, occurrenceRef fields
- trunkEvidence/leafEvidence/barkEvidence removed from dwc.occurrence
- Migration scripts create AC records (one per photo) linked to occurrence records
- Frontend displays tree photos and species images from AC records
- All media blobs are naturally atomic (one blob per record, no GC risk)
close_reason: cf11b77 all 9 children closed, 5 reviewer bugs fixed

title: Rewrite measured tree migration script to create AC multimedia records
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-measured-trees-to-ac-records.js (create)

## What to do
Create a NEW migration script that creates `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` records for measured tree photos instead of writing evidence fields on `dwc.occurrence`.

This script replaces `migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js` (from 40.3/40.12). The old script should NOT be modified or deleted — it stays as a reference.

### Architecture
For each tree with photos:
1. The `dwc.occurrence` record already exists (or will be created) with `basisOfRecord: 'HumanObservation'` and `dynamicProperties.dataType: 'measuredTree'`. The occurrence record does NOT get any evidence blob fields.
2. For each photo (trunk, leaf, bark), create a separate `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` record with:
   - `occurrenceRef`: AT-URI of the dwc.occurrence record (`at://<did>/app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence/<rkey>`)
   - `subjectPart`: `'entireOrganism'` for trunk photos, `'leaf'` for leaf photos, `'bark'` for bark photos
   - `file`: compressed blob (800px wide WebP q80 via sharp)
   - `format`: `'image/webp'`
   - `accessUri`: original S3/Kobo URL (full-res reference)
   - `variantLiteral`: `'Medium Quality'`
   - `createdAt`: ISO timestamp

### Flow per tree feature
1. Extract photo URLs from GeoJSON properties (same logic as existing script: `extractPhotoUrls()`)
2. Compute deterministic occurrence rkey: `makeOccurrenceRkey(orgDid, lat, lon, species)` — same as existing script
3. Check if occurrence record exists; if not, create it (same fields as existing script, but WITHOUT any evidence blob fields)
4. For each photo URL (trunk, leaf, bark) that is not null and not a Google Drive URL:
   a. Download the image
   b. Compress to 800px WebP q80
   c. `uploadBlob` to PDS
   d. Immediately `createRecord` for `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` referencing the blob — this is the atomic pattern that prevents blob GC
   e. Delete local temp file
5. Create `dwc.measurement` records for DBH and height (same as existing script)

### Idempotency
- Use deterministic rkeys for AC records: `ac${sha256(occurrenceRkey + '::' + subjectPart).slice(0, 20)}`
- Before creating, check if AC record with that rkey already exists
- Progress tracking: `scripts/migrations/data/ac-measured-trees-progress.json`
- Error log: `scripts/migrations/data/ac-measured-trees-errors.json`

### CLI interface
Same as existing script:
```
node scripts/migrations/migrate-measured-trees-to-ac-records.js   [--service https://climateai.org]   [--repos-file tmp/pds-repo-inventory-*.json]   [--credentials tmp/pds-credentials.json]   [--handle handle.climateai.org]   [--limit N]   [--dry-run]   [--out tmp/ac-measured-trees-migration-results.json]
```

### Key differences from old script
- Does NOT write `trunkEvidence`/`leafEvidence`/`barkEvidence` on occurrence records
- Creates separate `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` records instead
- Each photo = one `uploadBlob` + one `createRecord` (naturally atomic)
- AC record rkeys are deterministic based on occurrence rkey + subjectPart

### Reuse from existing script
Copy these helper functions verbatim from `migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js`:
- `parseArgs()` (update defaults for new file paths)
- `loadInventory()`, `loadCredentials()`
- `loadProgress()`, `saveProgress()`, `loadErrors()`, `appendError()`
- `fetchBinaryWithRetry()`, `fetchBinary()`, `fetchJson()`
- `getSharp()`, `compressImage()`
- `makeOccurrenceRkey()`, `makeMeasurementRkey()`
- `extractSpecies()`, `extractEventDate()`, `extractDbh()`, `extractHeight()`, `extractPhotoUrls()`, `isGoogleDriveUrl()`
- `listSiteRecords()`, `fetchPdsBlob()`
- `listExistingOccurrenceRkeys()`, `listExistingMeasurementRkeys()`

## Don't
- Don't modify the existing `migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js`
- Don't modify any lexicon files or frontend code
- Don't use `putRecord` to update occurrence records with evidence fields — the whole point is that evidence goes in AC records
- Don't buffer multiple blobs in memory — stream one at a time (12GB disk constraint)
acceptance_criteria:
1. scripts/migrations/migrate-measured-trees-to-ac-records.js exists
2. Script creates app.gainforest.ac.multimedia records (not evidence fields on occurrence)
3. Each AC record has: occurrenceRef (at-uri), subjectPart (entireOrganism|leaf|bark), file (blob), format, accessUri, variantLiteral, createdAt
4. Blob upload is immediately followed by createRecord (atomic pattern)
5. Deterministic rkeys: ac + sha256(occurrenceRkey + '::' + subjectPart).slice(0, 20)
6. --dry-run flag works (no PDS writes)
7. Progress file at scripts/migrations/data/ac-measured-trees-progress.json
8. bun run lint passes
close_reason: f5365f3 feat: create AC multimedia migration script for measured tree photos

title: Rewrite species image migration script to create AC multimedia records
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js (create)

## What to do
Create a NEW migration script that creates `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` records for species prediction images instead of writing `imageEvidence` blobs on `dwc.occurrence`.

This script replaces `migrate-occurrence-images-to-blobs.js` (from 40.4/40.13). The old script should NOT be modified or deleted.

### Architecture
For each MachineObservation occurrence with a `speciesImageUrl` or `thumbnailUrl`:
1. The `dwc.occurrence` record already exists with `basisOfRecord: 'MachineObservation'`
2. Create one `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` record with:
   - `occurrenceRef`: AT-URI of the existing dwc.occurrence record
   - `subjectPart`: `'entireOrganism'` (species photos are always whole-organism)
   - `file`: compressed blob (800px wide WebP q80 via sharp)
   - `format`: `'image/webp'`
   - `accessUri`: original `speciesImageUrl` or `thumbnailUrl` (full-res reference)
   - `variantLiteral`: `'Medium Quality'`
   - `createdAt`: ISO timestamp

### Flow per occurrence
1. List all `dwc.occurrence` records for the org
2. Filter to `basisOfRecord === 'MachineObservation'` with `speciesImageUrl` or `thumbnailUrl` present
3. For each qualifying occurrence:
   a. Check if an AC record already exists for this occurrence (deterministic rkey)
   b. Download the image from `speciesImageUrl` (or `thumbnailUrl` fallback)
   c. Compress to 800px WebP q80
   d. `uploadBlob` to PDS
   e. Immediately `createRecord` for `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` (atomic pattern)

### Idempotency
- Deterministic rkeys: `ac${sha256(occurrenceUri + '::entireOrganism').slice(0, 20)}`
  where `occurrenceUri` is the full AT-URI of the occurrence record
- Progress tracking: `scripts/migrations/data/ac-species-images-progress.json`
- Error log: `scripts/migrations/data/ac-species-images-errors.json`

### CLI interface
```
node scripts/migrations/migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js   [--service https://climateai.org]   [--repos-file tmp/pds-repo-inventory-*.json]   [--credentials tmp/pds-credentials.json]   [--handle handle.climateai.org]   [--limit N]   [--dry-run]   [--out tmp/ac-species-images-migration-results.json]
```

### Key differences from old script
- Does NOT use `putRecord` to update occurrence records with `imageEvidence`
- Creates separate `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` records instead
- Each image = one `uploadBlob` + one `createRecord` (naturally atomic)
- Keeps `speciesImageUrl`/`thumbnailUrl` strings on occurrence records (backward compat)

### Reuse from existing scripts
Copy helper functions from `migrate-occurrence-images-to-blobs.js` and/or `migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js`:
- `parseArgs()`, `loadInventory()`, `loadCredentials()`
- `loadProgress()`, `saveProgress()`, `loadErrors()`, `appendError()`
- `fetchBinaryWithRetry()`, `fetchBinary()`, `sleep()`
- `getSharp()`, `compressImage()`

## Don't
- Don't modify the existing `migrate-occurrence-images-to-blobs.js`
- Don't modify any `dwc.occurrence` records (no putRecord calls)
- Don't modify any lexicon files or frontend code
- Don't buffer multiple blobs in memory
acceptance_criteria:
1. scripts/migrations/migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js exists
2. Script creates app.gainforest.ac.multimedia records (not imageEvidence on occurrence)
3. Each AC record has: occurrenceRef (at-uri), subjectPart ('entireOrganism'), file (blob), format, accessUri, variantLiteral, createdAt
4. Blob upload is immediately followed by createRecord (atomic pattern)
5. Deterministic rkeys based on occurrence URI + subjectPart
6. --dry-run flag works (no PDS writes)
7. Progress file at scripts/migrations/data/ac-species-images-progress.json
8. bun run lint passes

title: Fix: appendError arg order swapped in migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js (from green_globe-46.6)
description: Review of green_globe-46.6 found: migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js defines appendError(errorEntry, errors) but migrate-measured-trees-to-ac-records.js defines appendError(errors, entry). The species images script has the arguments in the opposite order from the measured trees script. The species images script's appendError does NOT write to disk (no fs.writeFileSync call), so errors are only held in memory and lost if the process crashes. Evidence: species script line 212: 'function appendError(errorEntry, errors)' — no disk write. Measured trees script line 162: 'function appendError(errors, entry)' — writes to disk. Fix: add disk persistence to appendError in migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js (or standardize both scripts to the same signature and behavior).
close_reason: f7e7bf2 Fix: standardize appendError arg order and add disk persistence in species images migration

title: Fix: species images migration does not guard login behind --dry-run (from green_globe-46.6)
description: Review of green_globe-46.6 found: migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js always calls agent.login() regardless of --dry-run flag (line ~564). The measured trees script (46.5) correctly wraps login in 'if (!args.dryRun)'. In the species images script, running --dry-run still requires valid credentials and attempts a real PDS login, which defeats the purpose of dry-run testing. Evidence: species script main() has no dryRun guard around agent.login(); measured trees script line 761: 'if (!args.dryRun) { await agent.login(...) }'. Fix: wrap agent.login() in 'if (!args.dryRun)' in migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js.
close_reason: 8fffe28 Fix: guard agent.login() behind !dryRun in species images migration

title: Fix: species images migration missing $type field on AC record (from green_globe-46.6)
description: Review of green_globe-46.6 found: migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js constructs the AC record object without a $type field (lines 398-406). The measured trees script (46.5) correctly sets '$type: AC_MULTIMEDIA_COLLECTION' on the record. ATProto PDS createRecord calls should include $type for proper lexicon validation. Evidence: species script acRecord object at line 398 has no $type key. Measured trees script line 672: '$type: AC_MULTIMEDIA_COLLECTION'. Fix: add '$type: AC_MULTIMEDIA_COLLECTION' to the acRecord object in migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js.
close_reason: 106bccf Fix: add $type field to AC record in species images migration

title: Fix: layer.json name and description fields missing maxGraphemes (from green_globe-1.5)
description: Review of green_globe-1.5 found that organization/layer.json has two pre-existing string fields without maxGraphemes constraints: (1) 'name' field: type string, no maxGraphemes, no format — the spec says this is the layer name but no length constraint was added. (2) 'description' field: type string, no maxGraphemes, no format. AC9 requires 'all string fields have maxGraphemes constraints'. These are pre-existing fields that the task did not fix. All new fields added by the task (category, opacity, unit, minValue, maxValue, tilePattern, bounds, dataSource, dataDate, propertyKey) do have correct maxGraphemes constraints. The legendEntry def fields also have correct constraints.

title: Fix: fetchMultimediaIndex duplicated in 3 files — extract to shared module (from green_globe-46)
description: Review of green_globe-46 found: fetchMultimediaIndex is copy-pasted in three files: use-organization-measured-trees.ts (lines 112-151), use-organization-occurrences.ts (lines 34-68), and Predictions/store/utils.ts (lines 30-64). The measured-trees variant returns MultimediaByOccurrence (grouped by subjectPart), while the other two return MultimediaIndex (Map<string, string>, first image only). The RawMultimediaValue and RawMultimediaRecord types are also duplicated across all three files. This is the same pattern that was fixed for extractCid in 46.4. The duplication means any future change to the AC multimedia fetch logic must be applied in three places. Suggest extracting to @/lib/atproto/ac-multimedia.ts with both variants (or a single flexible version). This is a maintainability issue, not a correctness bug, but should be tracked.
close_reason: 0fd8cd7 extract fetchMultimediaIndex to shared ac-multimedia module

title: Fix: fetchMultimediaIndex not fetched in parallel with occurrences in use-organization-occurrences.ts (from green_globe-46.8)
description: Review of green_globe-46.8 found: in use-organization-occurrences.ts, fetchMultimediaIndex(did) is awaited sequentially before the occurrence pagination loop starts (line 332: 'const multimediaIndex = await fetchMultimediaIndex(did)'). The comment says 'in parallel with the first page of occurrences' but it is actually sequential — the multimedia fetch completes fully before any occurrence records are fetched. By contrast, use-organization-measured-trees.ts correctly uses Promise.all([fetchMeasurementIndex, fetchMultimediaIndex, occurrencesFetch]) to run all three in parallel. The same sequential issue exists in Predictions/store/utils.ts (line 317). Fix: wrap fetchMultimediaIndex and the occurrence listRecords in a Promise.all in both files, matching the pattern in use-organization-measured-trees.ts.
close_reason: 358d319 fix: fetch multimediaIndex in parallel with occurrences

title: Epic: Migrate Oceanus Google Drive photos to PDS AC multimedia records
description: ## Goal Migrate 2,996 unique Google Drive-hosted tree photos from Oceanus Conservation (oceanus-conservati.climateai.org, DID did:plc:6oxtzu7gxz7xcldvtwfh3bpt) to the PDS at climateai.org as app.gainforest.ac.multimedia records. These photos were skipped during the initial measured-trees migration because the script did not support Google Drive URLs (logged with reason: google-drive-url). ## Success - All 2,996 unique Google Drive images downloaded, compressed (800px WebP q80), uploaded as blobs to PDS - Each image has a corresponding app.gainforest.ac.multimedia record linked to its existing dwc.occurrence via occurrenceRef - Script is idempotent (deterministic rkeys), resumable (progress JSON), disk-safe (one image at a time, 12GB constraint) - Dry-run mode works correctly (no PDS writes, no progress file mutations) ## Constraints - Single org only: oceanus-conservati.climateai.org - Source data: scripts/migrations/data/ac-measured-trees-errors.json (15,885 entries with reason google-drive-url across 5 duplicate site rkeys, mapping to 2,996 unique GDrive file IDs) - All entries are photoType trunk (maps to subjectPart entireOrganism) - 3,189 dwc.occurrence records already exist on PDS (created by measured-trees migration) - Must follow existing script patterns from migrate-measured-trees-to-ac-records.js and migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js

title: Create migrate-oceanus-gdrive-to-ac-records.js script
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-oceanus-gdrive-to-ac-records.js (create)

## Reference files (read, do NOT modify)
- scripts/migrations/migrate-measured-trees-to-ac-records.js — copy patterns for: parseArgs, progress/error tracking, compressImage, fetchBinary/fetchBinaryWithRetry, fetchPdsBlob, deterministic rkey functions (makeOccurrenceRkey, makeAcMultimediaRkey), extractSpecies, listSiteRecords, listExistingAcMultimediaRkeys, PDS login, cooldown, tmpDir cleanup
- scripts/migrations/migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js — copy patterns for: processOccurrenceRecord flow, COOLDOWN_MS, per-item progress save
- lexicons/app/gainforest/ac/multimedia.json — AC multimedia schema (required fields: subjectPart, file, createdAt)
- scripts/migrations/data/ac-measured-trees-errors.json — source of Google Drive URLs (15,885 entries, 2,996 unique file IDs)

## What to do
Create a Node.js CJS migration script. Use eslint-disable comments for require() calls (same pattern as reference scripts).

### 1. Error log parsing and deduplication
Read scripts/migrations/data/ac-measured-trees-errors.json. Filter entries where reason === 'google-drive-url'. All 15,885 entries are from oceanus-conservati.climateai.org (DID did:plc:6oxtzu7gxz7xcldvtwfh3bpt), all photoType 'trunk'. Extract Google Drive file ID from each URL via: url.match(/[?&]id=([^&]+)/)[1]. Group entries by siteRkey (there are 5 unique site rkeys, each with 3,177 entries — they are duplicate GeoJSON sites).

### 2. Occurrence resolution (GeoJSON fetch)
For each unique siteRkey: fetch the site record from PDS via agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord({repo: did, collection: 'app.gainforest.organization.site', rkey: siteRkey}). From the site record value, get the trees GeoJSON blob — same pattern as measured-trees lines 808-853: extract blobCid from value.trees.blob.ref.$link or value.trees.ref.$link, fetch via fetchPdsBlob(service, did, blobCid). Fallback to S3: https://gainforest-transparency-dashboard.s3.amazonaws.com/shapefiles/oceanus-conservati-all-tree-plantings.geojson. Cache GeoJSON per siteRkey (Map). For each error entry: look up geojson.features[featureIdx]. Extract [lon, lat] = feature.geometry.coordinates. Extract species via extractSpecies(feature.properties) — copy this function verbatim from measured-trees. Compute occurrenceRkey = makeOccurrenceRkey(did, lat, lon, species) — copy this function verbatim. Build occurrenceAtUri = 'at://' + did + '/app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence/' + occurrenceRkey.

### 3. Deduplication
Build the work list as a Map keyed by gdriveFileId + '::' + occurrenceRkey. Each value: {gdriveFileId, occurrenceRkey, occurrenceAtUri, subjectPart, originalUrl, siteRkey, featureIdx}. Map photoType to subjectPart: trunk->entireOrganism, leaf->leaf, bark->bark. Log dedup stats: 'N error entries -> M unique file IDs -> K work items'. Skip entries where featureIdx is out of bounds or feature lacks coordinates (log as error).

### 4. Pre-check existing AC records
List existing AC multimedia rkeys on PDS using listExistingAcMultimediaRkeys(agent, did) — copy from measured-trees lines 585-606. Compute acRkey = makeAcMultimediaRkey(occurrenceRkey, subjectPart) for each work item. Skip items whose acRkey already exists. Also optionally verify occurrence exists: list existing occurrence rkeys via listExistingOccurrenceRkeys(agent, did) — warn (do not fail) if occurrence missing.

### 5. Google Drive download
Convert URL: replace 'drive.google.com/open?id=XXX' with 'drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id=XXX'. Also handle 'drive.google.com/file/d/XXX/view' format: extract XXX, build uc URL. CRITICAL: Modify fetchBinary() to also follow HTTP 303 redirects. The existing code handles 301, 302, 307, 308 — add 303. Google Drive returns 303 See Other redirecting to drive.usercontent.google.com. Use fetchBinaryWithRetry() with 3 retries and exponential backoff (copy from measured-trees).

### 6. Compress and upload
For each work item (sequential, one at a time): download image, write to tmpDir (os.tmpdir), compress via compressImage() to 800px wide WebP quality 80 (copy from measured-trees), uploadBlob to PDS (agent.uploadBlob(compressed, {encoding: 'image/webp'})), immediately putRecord for AC multimedia (atomic pattern prevents blob GC). Delete temp file after upload. AC record shape: {$type: 'app.gainforest.ac.multimedia', occurrenceRef: occurrenceAtUri, subjectPart: subjectPart, file: blobRef, format: 'image/webp', accessUri: originalGdriveUrl, variantLiteral: 'Medium Quality', createdAt: nowIso}. Use putRecord (not createRecord) with the deterministic rkey for idempotency. Add 1000ms cooldown between PDS write operations.

### 7. Progress and error tracking
Progress file: scripts/migrations/data/oceanus-gdrive-progress.json. Structure: {completedKeys: string[]} where each key is the dedup key (gdriveFileId + '::' + occurrenceRkey). After each successful AC record creation, add key to completedKeys and call saveProgress(). On resume, skip work items whose key is in the completedKeys set. CRITICAL: --dry-run must NOT call saveProgress() or write to the progress file. Guard every saveProgress() call with if (!args.dryRun). Error file: scripts/migrations/data/oceanus-gdrive-errors.json. Use appendError() pattern from measured-trees — append entry, write full array after each error.

### 8. CLI arguments
Copy parseArgs() pattern. Flags: --service (default https://climateai.org), --credentials (default tmp/pds-credentials.json), --handle (default oceanus-conservati.climateai.org — validate error entries match this handle), --limit N (limit work items processed, not error entries), --dry-run (no PDS writes, no progress writes), --out (default tmp/oceanus-gdrive-migration-results.json). No --repos-file needed (DID is known from error log).

### 9. Logging
Use prefix [oceanus-gdrive] for all console output. Log: arg summary, dedup stats, per-item download/compress/upload status, final summary (created/skipped/errors counts).

### 10. Blob ref caching (optimization)
Cache gdriveFileId -> blobRef after first upload. If the same file ID appears with different occurrence rkeys, reuse the cached blobRef instead of re-downloading and re-uploading. This saves bandwidth for duplicate images across different occurrences.

## Don't
- Do NOT create or modify dwc.occurrence records (they already exist)
- Do NOT create dwc.measurement records
- Do NOT modify any existing files (error log, other scripts, lexicons)
- Do NOT process entries from other orgs (only oceanus-conservati.climateai.org)
- Do NOT use createRecord for AC records (use putRecord for idempotency)
- Do NOT write progress file during --dry-run
- Do NOT download in parallel (sequential one-at-a-time for 12GB disk constraint)
- Do NOT add 303 handling via a separate HTTP library — modify the fetchBinary redirect check inline
acceptance_criteria:
1. node --check scripts/migrations/migrate-oceanus-gdrive-to-ac-records.js exits 0 (syntactically valid)
2. bun run lint passes with no new errors
3. fetchBinary() handles HTTP 303 redirects: the status code check includes 303 alongside 301 302 307 308
4. GDrive URL conversion: code converts drive.google.com/open?id=XXX to drive.google.com/uc?export=download&id=XXX
5. makeOccurrenceRkey and makeAcMultimediaRkey functions are identical to their counterparts in migrate-measured-trees-to-ac-records.js (same hash algorithm sha256, same prefix mt/ac, same input format, same slice length 20)
6. photoType to subjectPart mapping: trunk->entireOrganism, leaf->leaf, bark->bark
7. AC record has all required and expected fields: $type (app.gainforest.ac.multimedia), occurrenceRef (at-uri string), subjectPart, file (blob ref), format (image/webp), accessUri (original GDrive URL), variantLiteral (Medium Quality), createdAt
8. Every saveProgress() call is guarded by !args.dryRun (grep for saveProgress in the file shows zero unguarded calls)
9. Script accepts --service --credentials --handle --limit --dry-run --out flags via parseArgs
10. Dedup key is gdriveFileId + '::' + occurrenceRkey (work items are deduplicated by this composite key before processing)
11. Script uses putRecord (not createRecord) for AC multimedia records
12. Cooldown of at least 1000ms between PDS write operations
13. Temp files are cleaned up after each image (written to os.tmpdir, deleted in finally block)
close_reason: 4a33caf feat: add Oceanus Google Drive to AC multimedia migration script

title: Epic: Migrate auth from BrowserOAuthClient to server-side gainforest-sdk/oauth
description:
## Goal
Migrate green_globe authentication from the current client-side BrowserOAuthClient pattern to the server-side gainforest-sdk/oauth pattern used by bumicerts-platform-staging. This aligns both apps on a single auth architecture with server-side session management, Supabase-backed token storage, and iron-session encrypted cookies.

## Context
Currently green_globe uses @atproto/oauth-client-browser which stores OAuth tokens in IndexedDB and manages the entire flow in the browser. The target architecture (from bumicerts) moves all token handling server-side:
- OAuth tokens stored in Supabase (atproto_oauth_session, atproto_oauth_state tables)
- Lightweight encrypted cookie via iron-session holds only { did, handle, isLoggedIn }
- Server actions handle authorize, logout, checkSession, getProfile
- OAuth callback is a server-side API route
- Client metadata and JWKS served via dynamic API routes
- ES256 JWK private key for production OAuth client authentication
- Auth state on client via Zustand store (not React Context)

## Scope
- Add new env vars (Supabase, cookie secret, JWK private key)
- Create server-side ATProto SDK configuration (lib/atproto.ts)
- Create 3 API routes: /api/oauth/callback, /client-metadata.json, /.well-known/jwks.json
- Create server actions file for auth operations
- Create Zustand auth store
- Rewrite AtprotoProvider to use server actions + Zustand
- Update all 3 consumers of useAtproto to use Zustand store
- Remove old BrowserOAuthClient provider, /callback page, old static client-metadata.json
- Remove @atproto/oauth-client-browser dependency
- Add jose, @supabase/supabase-js dependencies
- Copy JWK generation script from bumicerts
- Update .env.example

## Key constraints
- The gainforest-sdk vendor tgz is already in place and exports gainforest-sdk/oauth
- The existing unauthenticated ClimateAIAgent at src/lib/atproto/agent.ts must NOT be touched
- The existing gainforest-sdk.ts and gainforest-sdk.server.ts config files stay; the server config TODO gets resolved in a follow-up
- The admin invite codes system at /invitecodes is unrelated and must NOT be touched
- Green globe runs on port 8910 in dev (not 3000)
- All auth consumers currently use useAtproto() hook from React Context; they must migrate to Zustand store
close_reason: 2cb9e7d auth migration complete — BrowserOAuthClient replaced with server-side gainforest-sdk/oauth, verified working in dev

```
# Supabase (for OAuth session storage)

```

### 3. Create scripts/generate-jwk.ts

title: Full build verification of auth migration
description:
## Files

## What to do
Run the full quality gate suite to verify the auth migration compiles and builds correctly.

### Steps
1. Run `bun run lint` — must pass with zero errors
2. Run `bun run build` — must complete successfully
   - Note: build may warn about missing env vars (SUPABASE_URL, COOKIE_SECRET, etc.) since they won't be in .env during CI. This is acceptable. What matters is that TypeScript compilation succeeds and there are no import errors.
   - If build fails due to missing env vars being required at module scope (e.g. the `throw new Error('ATPROTO_JWK_PRIVATE is not set')` in sdk.ts), wrap the throw in a check: only throw when the module is actually used at runtime, not at import time. File a follow-up issue for the fix if needed.

title: Create server-side ATProto SDK configuration (lib/atproto.ts)
description:
## Files
- src/lib/atproto/sdk.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a new file `src/lib/atproto/sdk.ts` that configures the ATProto SDK for server-side OAuth. This is the green_globe equivalent of bumicerts' `lib/atproto.ts`.

### Implementation
The file must:

1. Import and configure Supabase client with service role key (server-side only)
2. Define `APP_ID = "greenglobe"` (not "bumicerts")
3. Export `OAUTH_SCOPE = "atproto transition:generic"`
4. Export `resolvePublicUrl()` function that resolves the app's public URL:
   - Check `VERCEL_BRANCH_URL` first (for Vercel preview deploys)
   - Then `VERCEL_URL`
   - Then fall back to `http://127.0.0.1:8910` in dev (NOTE: port 8910, not 3000)
   - Throw if none found in production
5. Export dev OAuth config (`DEV_OAUTH_CONFIG`): loopback client per RFC 8252
   - client_id: `http://localhost?scope=...&redirect_uri=http://127.0.0.1:8910/api/oauth/callback`
   - redirectUri: `${PUBLIC_URL}/api/oauth/callback`
   - jwksUri: `${PUBLIC_URL}/.well-known/jwks.json`
   - jwkPrivate from `ATPROTO_JWK_PRIVATE` env var
6. Export prod OAuth config (`PROD_OAUTH_CONFIG`):
   - client_id: `${PUBLIC_URL}/client-metadata.json`
   - redirectUri, jwksUri, jwkPrivate same pattern
7. Export `atprotoSDK` created via `createATProtoSDK()` from `gainforest-sdk/oauth`:
   - oauth: dev or prod config based on `NODE_ENV`
   - servers.pds: `https://${allowedPDSDomains[0]}` (import from `@/config/gainforest-sdk`)
   - storage.sessionStore: `createSupabaseSessionStore(supabase, APP_ID)`
   - storage.stateStore: `createSupabaseStateStore(supabase, APP_ID)`

### Reference file
Follow the exact pattern from `C:\Users\infec\MyFolders\Documents\Work\Gainforest\bumicerts-platform-staging\lib\atproto.ts` but adapt:
- APP_ID → "greenglobe"
- Port → 8910
- Import `allowedPDSDomains` from `@/config/gainforest-sdk`

## Don't
- Touch `src/lib/atproto/agent.ts` (the unauthenticated ClimateAIAgent)
- Create this file at `src/lib/atproto.ts` — it must be at `src/lib/atproto/sdk.ts` to colocate with the existing atproto directory
- Import from or modify `src/config/gainforest-sdk.server.ts`
acceptance_criteria:
1. File exists at src/lib/atproto/sdk.ts
2. Exports: OAUTH_SCOPE, resolvePublicUrl, DEV_OAUTH_CONFIG, PROD_OAUTH_CONFIG, atprotoSDK
3. APP_ID is 'greenglobe'
4. Dev redirect URI uses port 8910
5. Imports createATProtoSDK, createSupabaseSessionStore, createSupabaseStateStore from gainforest-sdk/oauth
6. Imports allowedPDSDomains from @/config/gainforest-sdk
7. `bun run lint` passes
8. src/lib/atproto/agent.ts is NOT modified

title: Create OAuth API routes (callback, client-metadata, JWKS)
description:
## Files
- src/app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (create)
- src/app/client-metadata.json/route.ts (create)
- src/app/.well-known/jwks.json/route.ts (create)

## What to do
Create three API routes that handle the server-side OAuth flow. All three follow the exact patterns from bumicerts-platform-staging.

### 1. src/app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts
Export a `GET` handler that:
- Reads query params from the request (`code`, `state`)
- Calls `atprotoSDK.callback(searchParams)` to exchange the authorization code for an OAuth session
- Resolves the handle from DID via `agent.com.atproto.repo.describeRepo({ repo: session.did })`
- Saves app session via `saveAppSession({ did: session.did, handle: repo.handle, isLoggedIn: true })`
- On success: `redirect("/")`
- On failure: `redirect("/?error=auth_failed")`
- Import `atprotoSDK` from `@/lib/atproto/sdk`
- Import `saveAppSession`, `Agent` from `gainforest-sdk/oauth`
- Import `redirect` from `next/navigation`, `NextRequest` from `next/server`
- IMPORTANT: `redirect()` throws a control-flow exception, so it must be called OUTSIDE the try/catch. Use a `success` boolean flag pattern (see bumicerts reference).

### 2. src/app/client-metadata.json/route.ts
Export a `GET` handler that returns OAuth client metadata JSON. Must handle dev vs prod:
- Dev: loopback client (RFC 8252) with `application_type: "native"`, `token_endpoint_auth_method: "none"`
- Prod: web client with `application_type: "web"`, `token_endpoint_auth_method: "private_key_jwt"`, `token_endpoint_auth_signing_alg: "ES256"`
- Both include: `dpop_bound_access_tokens: true`, `jwks_uri`, proper redirect_uris pointing to `/api/oauth/callback`
- `client_name: "GainForest"` (not "Bumicerts")
- Import `OAUTH_SCOPE`, `resolvePublicUrl` from `@/lib/atproto/sdk`
- Return with `Content-Type: application/json` and `Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600`

### 3. src/app/.well-known/jwks.json/route.ts
Export a `GET` handler that serves the public JWKS:
- Read `ATPROTO_JWK_PRIVATE` env var
- Parse JSON, extract keys array
- Strip private component (`d`), `use`, and `key_ops` from each key
- Add `key_ops: ["verify"]` for public key
- Return as JSON with `Content-Type: application/json` and `Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600`
- Return 500 with error message if env var is missing or invalid JSON

### Reference files
- `bumicerts-platform-staging/app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts`
- `bumicerts-platform-staging/app/client-metadata.json/route.ts`
- `bumicerts-platform-staging/app/.well-known/jwks.json/route.ts`

## Don't
- Touch any existing API routes (especially /api/verify-invite-password, /api/admin/invite-codes, /api/atproto/did-doc)
- Use any imports from the old atproto-provider.tsx
- Import BrowserOAuthClient
acceptance_criteria:

2. GET /api/oauth/callback handles the full OAuth callback flow (code exchange → handle resolution → session save → redirect)
3. GET /client-metadata.json returns valid JSON with correct dev/prod metadata including client_name 'GainForest'
4. GET /.well-known/jwks.json returns public keys with private components stripped

title: Create server actions for auth operations (authorize, logout, checkSession, getProfile)
description:
## Files
- src/app/_components/actions/oauth.ts (create)

## What to do
Create a server actions file with four exported async functions that handle all auth operations server-side. This replaces the client-side BrowserOAuthClient methods.

The file must start with `"use server";` directive.

### 1. `authorize(handle: string): Promise<{ authorizationUrl: string }>`
- Normalize handle: if it doesn't contain a dot, append `.${allowedPDSDomains[0]}`
- Call `atprotoSDK.authorize(normalizedHandle)`
- Return `{ authorizationUrl: authUrl.toString() }`

### 2. `logout(): Promise<{ success: boolean }>`
- Read session via `getAppSession()`
- If session has a DID: call `atprotoSDK.revokeSession(session.did)` in a try/catch (don't throw on failure)
- Always call `clearAppSession()` in the finally block
- Return `{ success: true }`

### 3. `checkSession(): Promise<{ authenticated: false } | { authenticated: true; did: string; handle?: string }>`
- Read session via `getAppSession()`
- If `!session.isLoggedIn || !session.did`: return `{ authenticated: false }`
- Try to restore OAuth session via `atprotoSDK.restoreSession(session.did)`
- If null or throws: call `clearAppSession()` and return `{ authenticated: false }`
- Return `{ authenticated: true, did: session.did, handle: session.handle }`

### 4. `getProfile(did: string): Promise<ProfileData | null>`
- Export type `ProfileData = { handle: string; displayName?: string; description?: string; avatar?: string }`
- Restore session via `atprotoSDK.restoreSession(did)`
- Get repository via `atprotoSDK.repository(session)`
- Fetch profile via `repo.profile.get()`
- Handle avatar: if string use as-is; if object (blob ref) convert via `getBlobUrl(did, profile.avatar, allowedPDSDomains[0])`
- On any error: call `clearAppSession()` and return null
- Return `{ handle, displayName, description, avatar }`

### Imports
```ts
import { atprotoSDK } from "@/lib/atproto/sdk";
import { getAppSession, clearAppSession, AppSessionData } from "gainforest-sdk/oauth";
import { getBlobUrl } from "gainforest-sdk/utilities/atproto";
import { allowedPDSDomains } from "@/config/gainforest-sdk";
```

### Reference file
Follow `bumicerts-platform-staging/components/actions/oauth.ts` exactly, adapting imports.

## Don't
- Add any client-side code or `"use client"` directive
- Import from the old atproto-provider.tsx
- Touch any existing files
acceptance_criteria:
1. File exists at src/app/_components/actions/oauth.ts
2. File starts with 'use server' directive
3. Exports four functions: authorize, logout, checkSession, getProfile
4. Exports type ProfileData
5. authorize() normalizes handle and returns authorizationUrl
6. logout() revokes session in Supabase and clears cookie
7. checkSession() validates session against Supabase and clears stale cookies
8. getProfile() fetches profile via SDK repository pattern
9. All imports use @/lib/atproto/sdk (not bumicerts paths)
10. `bun run lint` passes
