title: Epic: Tree data upload MVP with CSV and KoboToolbox support
description: Build an MVP upload feature where community members can upload tree observation data (CSV files or KoboToolbox exports) into the Green Globe platform. Data flows: CSV/Kobo file -> browser-side parsing -> column mapping (auto-detected for Kobo, manual for generic CSV) -> Zod validation -> authenticated PDS writes as dwc.occurrence + dwc.measurement records. UI is a multi-step wizard at /upload route. Success: a user uploads a CSV of tree observations, maps columns to Darwin Core fields, previews/validates, and writes records to PDS. The data then flows to GBIF via the Epic A publishing pipeline. Key constraints: papaparse for browser-side CSV parsing, Zod for validation, PDS lexicons are source of truth for record shapes, ATProto session required for writes, dark mode UI with shadcn/ui components.
close_reason: 5464eb4 all children closed, manual E2E testing passed

title: Zod schemas and shared types for tree data upload
description:
## Files
- src/lib/upload/types.ts (create)
- src/lib/upload/schemas.ts (create)

## What to do
Define shared types and Zod validation schemas for parsing and validating tree observation data from CSV/KoboToolbox before writing to PDS.

In src/lib/upload/types.ts:
- type ColumnMapping = { sourceColumn: string, targetField: string, transform?: (value: string) => string }
- type MappedRow = Record<string, string> (a row after column mapping, keys are target DwC field names)
- type ValidationResult = { valid: ValidatedRow[], errors: RowError[] }
- type ValidatedRow = { index: number, occurrence: OccurrenceInput, measurements: MeasurementInput[] }
- type RowError = { index: number, issues: { path: string, message: string }[] }
- type OccurrenceInput = { scientificName: string, eventDate: string, decimalLatitude: number, decimalLongitude: number, basisOfRecord?: string, vernacularName?: string, recordedBy?: string, locality?: string, country?: string, countryCode?: string, occurrenceRemarks?: string, habitat?: string, samplingProtocol?: string, kingdom?: string }
- type MeasurementInput = { measurementType: string, measurementValue: string, measurementUnit?: string, measurementMethod?: string }
- Export list of valid target fields: TARGET_FIELDS = [{ field: 'scientificName', label: 'Scientific Name', required: true, category: 'occurrence' }, { field: 'eventDate', label: 'Event Date', required: true, category: 'occurrence' }, { field: 'decimalLatitude', label: 'Latitude', required: true, category: 'occurrence' }, { field: 'decimalLongitude', label: 'Longitude', required: true, category: 'occurrence' }, { field: 'vernacularName', label: 'Common Name', required: false, category: 'occurrence' }, { field: 'recordedBy', label: 'Recorded By', required: false, category: 'occurrence' }, { field: 'locality', label: 'Locality', required: false, category: 'occurrence' }, { field: 'country', label: 'Country', required: false, category: 'occurrence' }, { field: 'occurrenceRemarks', label: 'Remarks', required: false, category: 'occurrence' }, { field: 'habitat', label: 'Habitat', required: false, category: 'occurrence' }, { field: 'height', label: 'Tree Height (m)', required: false, category: 'measurement' }, { field: 'dbh', label: 'DBH (cm)', required: false, category: 'measurement' }, { field: 'diameter', label: 'Diameter (cm)', required: false, category: 'measurement' }, { field: 'canopyCover', label: 'Canopy Cover (%%)', required: false, category: 'measurement' }] as const

In src/lib/upload/schemas.ts:
- Import z from 'zod'
- OccurrenceRowSchema = z.object: scientificName z.string().min(1), eventDate z.string().min(1) with .refine for ISO 8601 or common date formats (YYYY-MM-DD, MM/DD/YYYY, DD/MM/YYYY, YYYY), decimalLatitude z.coerce.number().min(-90).max(90), decimalLongitude z.coerce.number().min(-180).max(180), basisOfRecord z.string().optional().default('HumanObservation'), plus all optional occurrence fields as z.string().optional()
- MeasurementFields: height z.coerce.number().positive().optional(), dbh z.coerce.number().positive().optional(), diameter z.coerce.number().positive().optional(), canopyCover z.coerce.number().min(0).max(100).optional()
- TreeRowSchema = OccurrenceRowSchema.merge(z.object(MeasurementFields))
- Function: parseAndValidateRows(rows: Record<string, string>[]) => ValidationResult — iterates rows, safeParse each with TreeRowSchema, splits measurement fields from occurrence fields, returns { valid: ValidatedRow[], errors: RowError[] }. For each valid row, extract measurement columns that have values and create MeasurementInput objects: height -> { measurementType: 'tree height', measurementValue: String(val), measurementUnit: 'm' }, dbh -> { measurementType: 'DBH', measurementValue: String(val), measurementUnit: 'cm' }, diameter -> { measurementType: 'diameter', measurementValue: String(val), measurementUnit: 'cm' }, canopyCover -> { measurementType: 'canopy cover', measurementValue: String(val), measurementUnit: '%%' }

## Don't
- Don't import from PDS or ATProto modules
- Don't handle CSV parsing — takes already-parsed row objects
- Don't handle column mapping — takes already-mapped rows
- Don't use 'any'
acceptance_criteria: 1. OccurrenceRowSchema requires scientificName, eventDate, decimalLatitude, decimalLongitude. 2. Invalid lat/lon (>90, >180) is caught by schema. 3. Measurement fields are coerced from string to number. 4. parseAndValidateRows returns valid rows with separated occurrence and measurement data. 5. Error rows include row index and per-field error messages. 6. At least 8 unit tests covering: valid row, missing required field, invalid lat, invalid lon, valid measurements, empty measurements skipped, multiple errors per row, date format validation. 7. bun run lint passes.
close_reason: 2df0a59 Zod schemas and shared types for tree data upload

title: Fix: false positive KoboToolbox detection for standard Darwin Core CSV headers
description:
## Files
- src/lib/upload/kobo-mapper.ts (modify)
- src/lib/upload/kobo-mapper.test.ts (modify)
- src/app/upload/_components/ColumnMappingStep.tsx (modify)

## What to do

detectKoboFormat() in kobo-mapper.ts uses matchPattern() which does lower.includes(entry.pattern) matching. Generic patterns like 'date', 'latitude', 'longitude', 'locality', 'height', 'dbh' match standard Darwin Core headers (eventDate contains 'date', decimalLatitude contains 'latitude', etc.). This gives generic-trees.csv 6/9=0.667 confidence, far above the 0.3 threshold, falsely detecting it as KoboToolbox format.

Cascade: since isKobo=true, FileDropStep.handleContinue() uses koboResult.mappings instead of autoDetectMappings(). The Kobo mapper doesn't have patterns for camelCase DwC names (scientificName, vernacularName, recordedBy — no underscore), so these columns get 'Skip this column' in Step 2.

### Fix approach

1. Add a koboSpecific boolean flag to the KoboPattern type (default false).

2. Mark patterns that are genuinely KoboToolbox-specific as koboSpecific: true:
   - gps, geopoint (combined GPS fields)
   - _gps_latitude, _gps_longitude, gps_latitude, gps_longitude (prefixed GPS)
   - plant_name (Kobo's species field)
   - fcd-tree_records-tree_time (Kobo's date field)
   - _submission_time, submission_time (Kobo metadata)

3. Keep all other patterns as koboSpecific: false (these are generic field names that appear in any tree CSV).

4. In detectKoboFormat(), count strong matches (koboSpecific=true) separately. Change the detection condition from confidence >= 0.3 to: confidence >= 0.3 AND strongMatchCount >= 1

5. In ColumnMappingStep.tsx (lines 121-129), replace the inline heuristic with a call to detectKoboFormat(headers).isKobo — this unifies format detection across Step 1 and Step 2. Pass the detected format as a prop from the parent, or call detectKoboFormat in the component.

### Test updates

6. Add a new test case for standard Darwin Core headers: detectKoboFormat(['scientificName','eventDate','decimalLatitude','decimalLongitude','vernacularName','recordedBy','locality','height','dbh']) must return isKobo = false.

7. The existing 'generic CSV headers' test (line 59-67) uses truly unrelated headers (id, name, value, etc.) so it will still pass. But the NEW test with DwC headers is the critical one that was missing.

8. The existing 'real Kobo headers' test will still pass because Plant_Name and FCD-tree_records-tree_time are strong indicators.

9. The existing 'mixed Kobo + generic headers' test has GPS and Plant_Name which are strong indicators, so it will still pass.

## Don't
- Don't remove any existing Kobo patterns — they should still MATCH for mapping purposes, just not count as strong Kobo indicators
- Don't change the confidence threshold (0.3 is fine)
- Don't change the matchPattern() function's matching logic (exact + includes is fine for mapping). Only change how matches are COUNTED for detection.
- Don't pass format as prop through wizard state — keep detection local to the components that need it
acceptance_criteria: 1. detectKoboFormat(['scientificName','eventDate','decimalLatitude','decimalLongitude','vernacularName','recordedBy','locality','height','dbh']) returns isKobo=false. 2. detectKoboFormat(['Plant_Name','_GPS_latitude','_GPS_longitude','FCD-tree_records-tree_time','DBH','Height']) returns isKobo=true. 3. detectKoboFormat(['GPS','Plant_Name','DBH','Height','notes','id','category','description']) returns isKobo=true. 4. All 23 existing tests in kobo-mapper.test.ts still pass. 5. New test for standard DwC headers added and passes. 6. ColumnMappingStep.tsx uses detectKoboFormat instead of inline heuristic. 7. bun run lint passes. 8. bunx vitest passes (all unit tests).
close_reason: 32070b6 fix: prevent false positive KoboToolbox detection for Darwin Core CSV headers

title: CSV parser hook with papaparse
description:
## Files
- src/lib/upload/use-csv-parser.ts (create)

## What to do
Install papaparse and create a React hook for browser-side CSV parsing.

First: bun add papaparse && bun add -d @types/papaparse

Hook: useCsvParser() returns { parseFile, parsedData, headers, rowCount, error, isParsing, reset }

- parseFile(file: File): Promise<void> — Uses Papa.parse(file, { header: true, skipEmptyLines: true, dynamicTyping: false, encoding: 'UTF-8', complete, error }). Store results in state.
- parsedData: Record<string, string>[] | null — Array of row objects (header -> value). All values are strings (dynamicTyping: false) to let Zod handle coercion later.
- headers: string[] | null — Column names from first row.
- rowCount: number — Total row count (excluding header and empty lines).
- error: string | null — Parse error message if any.
- isParsing: boolean — True while parsing is in progress.
- reset(): void — Clears all state back to initial.

Edge cases:
- Handle UTF-8 BOM (byte order mark) — papaparse handles this by default.
- Large files: papaparse streams by default for files, so this should handle large CSVs without memory issues.
- Tab-separated values: papaparse auto-detects delimiter, so .tsv files work too.
- Empty file: set error to 'File is empty'.
- File with only headers (no data rows): set parsedData to [], rowCount to 0, no error.
- Duplicate column names: papaparse appends _1, _2 etc. This is fine for our use case.

This is a 'use client' hook — add the directive at top of file.

## Don't
- Don't validate data — just parse CSV to objects
- Don't handle column mapping — separate module
- Don't use csv-parse (server-side only) — use papaparse (browser-side)
- Don't use any state management library — just React useState
acceptance_criteria: 1. papaparse and @types/papaparse are installed. 2. Hook returns typed parsedData as Record<string, string>[] with all values as strings. 3. Headers are correctly extracted from first row. 4. Empty file produces error message. 5. isParsing is true during parse, false after. 6. reset() clears all state. 7. bun run lint passes.
close_reason: 0b2388c feat: CSV parser hook with papaparse

title: KoboToolbox column auto-detector
description:
## Files
- src/lib/upload/kobo-mapper.ts (create)

## What to do
Auto-detect KoboToolbox CSV exports and map their column names to Darwin Core target fields.

Import ColumnMapping from src/lib/upload/types.ts.

Functions:
- detectKoboFormat(headers: string[]) => { isKobo: boolean, confidence: number, mappings: ColumnMapping[] }
  - Scans headers against known KoboToolbox field patterns (case-insensitive matching)
  - confidence: 0-1 = number of matched headers / total headers. isKobo = confidence >= 0.3
  - Returns mappings for all recognized columns

- getKoboColumnMappings(headers: string[]) => ColumnMapping[]
  - Returns mappings for all headers that match known Kobo patterns. Unrecognized headers are not included.

Known KoboToolbox field patterns to map (case-insensitive, check for exact match and contains):
- Plant_Name, plant_name, Plant_name -> scientificName
- GPS, _GPS_latitude, geopoint -> special handling: Kobo GPS format is 'lat lon alt accuracy' in a single field. Transform: split by space, index 0 = lat, index 1 = lon. Map to BOTH decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude.
- _GPS_latitude, gps_latitude, latitude, Latitude -> decimalLatitude
- _GPS_longitude, gps_longitude, longitude, Longitude -> decimalLongitude
- DBH, dbh, diameter_breast_height, Diameter_breast_height -> dbh (measurement)
- Height, height, tree_height, Tree_height, Tree_Height -> height (measurement)
- FCD-tree_records-tree_time, date, Date, observation_date, survey_date -> eventDate
- species, Species, scientific_name -> scientificName
- _submission_time, submission_time -> eventDate (fallback, lower priority than explicit date fields)
- recorder, Recorder, recorded_by, Recorded_by, observer -> recordedBy
- diameter, Diameter -> diameter (measurement)
- canopy_cover, Canopy_cover, canopy -> canopyCover (measurement)
- site, Site, locality, Locality, location -> locality
- country, Country -> country
- notes, Notes, remarks, Remarks, comments -> occurrenceRemarks
- common_name, Common_name, vernacular_name, local_name -> vernacularName
- habitat, Habitat -> habitat

For the GPS single-field case: the ColumnMapping should have a transform function that extracts the latitude or longitude from the combined string. Create TWO mappings from one source column: one for lat (transform extracts index 0), one for lon (transform extracts index 1).

Reference real KoboToolbox field names from: scripts/migrations/migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js (uses: Height, height, diameter, species, dateMeasured, Plant_Name, DBH, FCD-tree_records-tree_time).

## Don't
- Don't parse CSV — takes pre-parsed headers as string[]
- Don't validate values — just map column names
- Don't handle file I/O
- Don't import from papaparse or csv-parse
acceptance_criteria: 1. detectKoboFormat correctly identifies KoboToolbox exports by column names with confidence score. 2. Kobo GPS single-field format is split into separate lat/lon mappings with transform functions. 3. Case-insensitive matching works (e.g., 'plant_name' and 'Plant_Name' both match). 4. Unrecognized headers are not included in mappings. 5. At least 6 unit tests: real Kobo headers from migration script, generic CSV headers (low confidence), GPS field splitting, case variations, empty headers, mixed Kobo+generic. 6. bun run lint passes.
close_reason: eae8806 KoboToolbox column auto-detector

title: Generic CSV column mapper
description:
## Files
- src/lib/upload/column-mapper.ts (create)

## What to do
Auto-detect and apply column mappings for generic CSV files (non-KoboToolbox). Provides best-effort mapping based on common field naming conventions.

Import ColumnMapping, MappedRow from src/lib/upload/types.ts.

Functions:
- autoDetectMappings(headers: string[]) => ColumnMapping[]
  - Case-insensitive matching against known patterns.
  - If multiple source columns match the same target, pick the first match.
  - Return only matched columns; unrecognized headers are excluded.

- applyMappings(rows: Record<string, string>[], mappings: ColumnMapping[]) => MappedRow[]
  - For each row, create a new object with target field names as keys.
  - Apply transform functions from mappings if present.
  - Source columns not in mappings are dropped.
  - If a source column value is empty/undefined, skip it (don't include empty string in output).

Known generic patterns (case-insensitive matching, order = priority):
Occurrence fields:
- lat, latitude, decimal_latitude, decimalLatitude, y -> decimalLatitude
- lon, lng, longitude, decimal_longitude, decimalLongitude, x -> decimalLongitude
- species, scientific_name, scientificName, taxon, taxon_name -> scientificName
- date, event_date, eventDate, observation_date, obs_date, survey_date, collected_date -> eventDate
- observer, recorded_by, recordedBy, recorder, collector -> recordedBy
- name, common_name, vernacular_name, vernacularName, local_name -> vernacularName
- notes, remarks, comments, observation_remarks, occurrenceRemarks -> occurrenceRemarks
- country, Country -> country
- locality, location, site, site_name, place -> locality
- habitat, Habitat, vegetation -> habitat

Measurement fields:
- dbh, DBH, diameter_breast_height, breast_height_diameter -> dbh
- height, tree_height, plant_height -> height
- diameter, trunk_diameter, stem_diameter -> diameter
- canopy, canopy_cover, crown_cover -> canopyCover

## Don't
- Don't handle KoboToolbox-specific logic (that is in kobo-mapper.ts)
- Don't parse CSV files
- Don't validate values — just remap column names
- Don't import from papaparse or any CSV library
acceptance_criteria: 1. autoDetectMappings handles common CSV column naming conventions (lat/lon/species/date). 2. Case-insensitive matching works. 3. applyMappings correctly remaps source columns to target field names. 4. Transform functions are applied when present in mappings. 5. Empty source values are excluded from output rows. 6. At least 5 unit tests: standard column names, mixed case, partial matches, applyMappings with transforms, empty values. 7. bun run lint passes.
close_reason: 9d36943 Generic CSV column mapper

title: PDS write service for occurrence records
description:
## Files
- src/lib/upload/pds-writer.ts (create)

## What to do
Service to write validated tree data as dwc.occurrence records to the ATProto PDS.

Import OccurrenceInput, MeasurementInput from src/lib/upload/types.ts. Import Agent from @atproto/api. Import PDS_ENDPOINT from src/config/atproto.ts. Import TID from @atproto/common (or generate TID manually — check how existing code does it in scripts/migrations/).

Functions:
- writeOccurrenceToPds(agent: Agent, did: string, occurrence: OccurrenceInput, projectRef?: string) => Promise<{ uri: string, rkey: string }>
  - Generate a TID for the rkey (record key). Check scripts/migrations/migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js for TID generation pattern — use @atproto/common TID.nextStr() or similar.
  - Build the record object matching the app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence lexicon (see lexicons/app/gainforest/dwc/occurrence.json):
    - basisOfRecord: occurrence.basisOfRecord or 'HumanObservation'
    - scientificName: occurrence.scientificName
    - eventDate: occurrence.eventDate
    - decimalLatitude: String(occurrence.decimalLatitude) (lexicon stores as string)
    - decimalLongitude: String(occurrence.decimalLongitude) (lexicon stores as string)
    - geodeticDatum: 'EPSG:4326'
    - kingdom: occurrence.kingdom or 'Plantae'
    - license: 'http://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/'
    - occurrenceID: crypto.randomUUID()
    - occurrenceStatus: 'present'
    - vernacularName: occurrence.vernacularName (if provided)
    - recordedBy: occurrence.recordedBy (if provided)
    - locality: occurrence.locality (if provided)
    - country: occurrence.country (if provided)
    - countryCode: occurrence.countryCode (if provided)
    - occurrenceRemarks: occurrence.occurrenceRemarks (if provided)
    - habitat: occurrence.habitat (if provided)
    - samplingProtocol: occurrence.samplingProtocol (if provided)
    - projectRef: projectRef (if provided)
    - createdAt: new Date().toISOString()
  - Call agent.com.atproto.repo.putRecord({ repo: did, collection: 'app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence', rkey, record })
  - Return { uri: response.data.uri, rkey }

- writeMeasurementsToPds(agent: Agent, did: string, occurrenceUri: string, measurements: MeasurementInput[]) => Promise<{ uri: string, type: string }[]>
  - For each measurement in array:
    - Generate TID for rkey
    - Build record matching app.gainforest.dwc.measurement lexicon:
      - occurrenceRef: occurrenceUri
      - measurementType: measurement.measurementType
      - measurementValue: measurement.measurementValue
      - measurementUnit: measurement.measurementUnit (if provided)
      - measurementMethod: measurement.measurementMethod (if provided)
      - measurementID: crypto.randomUUID()
      - createdAt: new Date().toISOString()
    - Call agent.com.atproto.repo.putRecord
  - Return array of { uri, type: measurement.measurementType }

- writeTreeRecord(agent: Agent, did: string, row: { occurrence: OccurrenceInput, measurements: MeasurementInput[] }, projectRef?: string) => Promise<{ occurrenceUri: string, measurementUris: string[] }>
  - Convenience function: calls writeOccurrenceToPds, then writeMeasurementsToPds with the returned URI
  - Returns the occurrence URI and all measurement URIs

Edge cases:
- Optional fields that are undefined or empty string should NOT be included in the record object (don't write undefined or empty string to PDS)
- The agent must have an active session (authenticated)
- decimalLatitude and decimalLongitude are numbers in OccurrenceInput but strings in the lexicon — convert with String()

## Don't
- Don't handle multimedia/photo upload (future task)
- Don't handle CSV parsing or validation — takes pre-validated OccurrenceInput
- Don't create the agent — takes it as parameter
- Don't handle batch progress — caller manages that
acceptance_criteria: 1. writeOccurrenceToPds creates a valid app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence record with all required fields. 2. writeMeasurementsToPds creates linked measurement records with correct occurrenceRef. 3. Optional undefined/empty fields are omitted from the record. 4. TID is generated for each rkey. 5. decimalLatitude/Longitude are converted to strings for the lexicon. 6. bun run lint passes.
close_reason: 0098f8f add PDS write service for occurrence records

title: Upload wizard page shell and Step 1: file drop
description:
## Files
- src/app/upload/page.tsx (create)
- src/app/upload/_components/UploadWizard.tsx (create)
- src/app/upload/_components/FileDropStep.tsx (create)

## What to do
Create the /upload route and the first step of the upload wizard: file selection and CSV parsing.

In src/app/upload/page.tsx (server component):
- Simple page that renders UploadWizard inside a centered layout container
- Page metadata: title 'Upload Tree Data'
- No 'use client' directive — this is a server component that renders a client component

In src/app/upload/_components/UploadWizard.tsx ('use client'):
- Manages wizard state: currentStep (1-4), file (File | null), parsedData, headers, mappings (ColumnMapping[]), validationResult
- Renders the current step component based on currentStep

- Props passed down to steps include state and setter callbacks

In src/app/upload/_components/FileDropStep.tsx ('use client'):
- Drag-and-drop zone: a dashed-border box that accepts .csv and .tsv files
- Also has a click-to-browse button (hidden file input triggered by button click)

- While parsing: show a spinner/loading state

- Auto-detect format: import detectKoboFormat from kobo-mapper.ts. If isKobo, show a badge 'KoboToolbox format detected'. If not, show 'Generic CSV'.
- If detectKoboFormat returns isKobo, auto-fill mappings using the Kobo mappings. Otherwise use autoDetectMappings from column-mapper.ts. Pass mappings up to wizard via callback.
- 'Continue to Column Mapping' button at bottom (disabled until file is parsed)

Styling: dark mode (the app always has dark class on body). Use shadcn/ui components: Card, CardHeader, CardContent, Button. Use Tailwind classes. Follow the existing component patterns — reference src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/Biodiversity/Observations/MeasuredTrees/ExportDialog.tsx for dialog/card styling patterns.

## Don't
- Don't implement Steps 2-4 UI — just placeholder divs
- Don't validate data — Step 1 only parses
- Don't write to PDS
- Don't add navigation links from other parts of the app to /upload (keep it accessible by direct URL for now)
acceptance_criteria: 1. /upload route renders the wizard. 2. Drag-and-drop zone accepts .csv and .tsv files. 3. Click-to-browse works via hidden file input. 4. File >10MB shows error. 5. After parsing, shows file name, row count, column count. 6. KoboToolbox format detection badge appears for Kobo CSVs. 7. Continue button is disabled until parsing completes. 8. Dark mode styling matches existing app. 9. bun run lint passes.

title: Upload wizard Step 2: column mapping UI
description:

- src/app/upload/_components/UploadWizard.tsx (modify — replace Step 2 placeholder)

In ColumnMappingStep.tsx ('use client'):

title: Upload wizard Step 3: preview and validation
description:

title: Upload wizard Step 4: confirm and write to PDS
description:
## Files
- src/app/upload/_components/UploadStep.tsx (create)
- src/app/upload/_components/UploadWizard.tsx (modify — replace Step 4 placeholder)

## What to do
Build the final step: write validated tree data to PDS with progress tracking.

In UploadStep.tsx ('use client'):
Props: { validRows: ValidatedRow[], onComplete: () => void, onBack: () => void }

Auth check:
- Before starting upload, check if user has an active ATProto session. Import the ATProto session/agent from the existing auth pattern (check src/lib/atproto/sdk.ts and how existing components get authenticated agents).
- If not signed in: show a message 'You must be signed in to upload data' with a sign-in button/link. Reference the SignInBlueskyDialog pattern in the codebase.
- If signed in: show the upload UI.

Upload flow (triggered on mount or by a 'Start Upload' button):
1. For each row in validRows (sequentially, not parallel):
   a. Call writeTreeRecord from pds-writer.ts (which writes occurrence + measurements)
   b. Update progress state: { current: number, total: number, successes: number, failures: number, currentRow: string }
   c. On success: increment successes
   d. On error: increment failures, store error for that row. Do NOT stop — continue with next row.
2. After all rows processed, show completion summary.

Progress UI:
- Progress bar (shadcn/ui Progress component or simple div with width percentage)
- Text: 'Uploading row X of Y...' with current species name
- Running tally: 'X succeeded, Y failed'
- Per-row status list (scrollable): each row shows species name + checkmark or X with error message
- Estimated time remaining (optional, nice-to-have)

Completion UI:
- Success: green banner 'Successfully uploaded X tree records to Green Globe'
- If some failed: amber banner 'X records uploaded, Y failed'
- Details: expandable list of failed rows with error messages
- 'Done' button that navigates to home (/) or resets the wizard for another upload
- 'Upload More Data' button that resets wizard to Step 1

Modify UploadWizard.tsx: replace Step 4 placeholder, wire up validRows and completion handler.

## Don't
- Don't handle multimedia/photo upload — future enhancement
- Don't retry failed rows automatically
- Don't upload in parallel — sequential to avoid PDS rate limiting
- Don't allow going back during upload (disable Back button once upload starts)
- Don't implement GBIF publishing trigger — that is a separate manual CLI step
acceptance_criteria: 1. Auth check prevents upload if not signed in. 2. Progress bar tracks upload progress. 3. Each row writes occurrence + measurements via writeTreeRecord. 4. Failed rows don't stop the batch. 5. Completion summary shows success/failure counts. 6. Failed rows list with error messages is visible. 7. Done button resets wizard or navigates away. 8. Back button disabled during active upload. 9. bun run lint passes.
close_reason: 9d7beea feat: upload wizard Step 4 confirm and write to PDS

title: Fix: Radix Select crashes on empty-string value in ColumnMappingStep (from green_globe-57.7)
description: Review of green_globe-57.7 found: ColumnMappingStep.tsx line 211 renders <SelectItem value=""> for the 'Skip this column' option. Radix UI @radix-ui/react-select explicitly throws a runtime error when a SelectItem has an empty string value: 'A <Select.Item /> must have a value prop that is not an empty string.' (confirmed in node_modules/@radix-ui/react-select/dist/index.js line 892). This will crash the column mapping step for every user. Fix: use a sentinel value like '__skip__' for the skip option, and treat that sentinel as 'no mapping' in handleSelectChange.
close_reason: 842b88a fix: use __skip__ sentinel for Radix Select skip option

title: Fix: PreviewStep row-index mismatch corrupts error highlighting when errors exist beyond row 20 (from green_globe-57.8)
description: Review of green_globe-57.8 found: PreviewStep.tsx uses displayIdx (0..19 for the preview slice) to look up errors in errorByIndex, but errorByIndex is keyed by the original row index from parseAndValidateRows (which can be 0..N-1 across the full dataset). When the first 20 rows are all valid and errors only appear in rows 21+, errorByIndex will never match displayIdx and no errors are shown in the preview. Conversely, if row 5 has an error and row 25 also has an error, displayIdx=4 will correctly show row 5's error but displayIdx=4 in the preview slice is actually row 5 — this only works by coincidence when the preview starts at row 0. The fix is to use the actual row index (e.g. mappedRows index before slicing) rather than the slice-local displayIdx when looking up errorByIndex.
close_reason: e38f119 fix: use actual row index for errorByIndex lookup in PreviewStep

title: Fix: occurrence.json and dwc/defs.json plantTraits duplicate plant trait fields (from green_globe-1.6 / green_globe-1.8)
description: Review of green_globe-1.6 and green_globe-1.8 found a second cross-task duplication. Task 1.8 defined a plantTraits def in dwc/defs.json with fields: woodDensity, maxHeight, stemDiameter, stemConduitDiameter, barkThickness, rootDepth, leafArea, specificLeafArea, seedMass, growthForm, leafType, dispersalMode, pollinationMode, edibleParts, economicUses, traitSource. Task 1.6 independently added plant trait fields inline on occurrence.json: woodDensity, treeMaxHeight (note: different name from plantTraits.maxHeight), stemDiameter, stemConduitDiameter, barkThickness, rootDepth, edibleParts, economicUses. This creates: (1) duplication of 6 fields between occurrence.json inline and dwc.defs#plantTraits; (2) naming inconsistency — occurrence.json uses 'treeMaxHeight' but dwc.defs#plantTraits uses 'maxHeight' for the same concept. The occurrence.json should reference dwc.defs#plantTraits via a ref field, or the shared def should be removed. This will cause confusion for SDK consumers.

title: Fix: PreviewStep renders bare Fragment in tbody causing React key warning and potential hydration issues (from green_globe-57.8)
description:
## Files
- src/app/upload/_components/PreviewStep.tsx (modify)

## What to do

Line 186 returns a bare Fragment (<>) wrapping two <tr> elements inside previewRows.map(). The key prop is on the inner <tr> (line 188) instead of the Fragment wrapper. React requires the key on the outermost element returned from a map callback.

Fix:
1. Import Fragment from react (add to the existing import on line 3: import { useMemo, useState, Fragment } from 'react')
2. Line 186: replace <> with <Fragment key={rowIndex}>
3. Line 188: remove key={rowIndex} from the <tr> element
4. Line 243: replace </> with </Fragment>

That is the entire fix — 4 line changes.

## Don't
- Don't restructure the table layout or change any other logic
- Don't touch the error expansion row (line 225 already has key={`${rowIndex}-errors`})
- Don't change any other component
acceptance_criteria: 1. No React key warning in browser console when loading PreviewStep with any CSV file. 2. Error rows still expand/collapse correctly when clicked. 3. bun run lint passes. 4. bunx vitest passes.
close_reason: f5cff9f fix: use Fragment with key prop in PreviewStep map callback

title: Fix: PreviewStep calls applyMappings twice per render wasting CPU on large CSVs (from green_globe-57.8)
description: Review of green_globe-57.8 found: PreviewStep.tsx calls applyMappings(parsedData, mappings) twice — once inside the useMemo at line 56 (to get mappedHeaders and run validation) and again in a separate useMemo at line 83 (to get mappedRows for display). Both memos have the same [parsedData, mappings] dependency array, so they recompute together. For a 10 MB CSV this doubles the mapping work on every re-render. Fix: compute mappedRows once inside the first useMemo and return it alongside validationResult and mappedHeaders, eliminating the second applyMappings call.
close_reason: f4d3b9a Already fixed in prior session — duplicate applyMappings call removed

title: Fix: autoDetectMappings maps any column named 'name' to vernacularName, shadowing scientificName (from green_globe-57.4)
description: Review of green_globe-57.4 found: column-mapper.ts line 19 includes 'name' as a pattern for the vernacularName target. This is overly broad — a column literally named 'name' in a generic CSV is far more likely to be the scientific name (or a person's name) than a vernacular/common name. The pattern 'name' will match before 'species'/'scientific_name' patterns if the column appears first in the header list, silently mapping the primary species identifier to the wrong field. This means users with a 'name' column will see it auto-mapped to 'Common Name' instead of 'Scientific Name', requiring manual correction and potentially causing confusion. Fix: remove the bare 'name' pattern from the vernacularName group, keeping only 'common_name', 'vernacular_name', 'vernacularname', 'local_name'.
close_reason: 8af75f1 Fix: remove bare 'name' pattern from vernacularName mappings

title: Fix: UploadStep useEffect missing uploadStarted in dependency array causes stale-closure re-trigger risk (from green_globe-57.9)
description: Review of green_globe-57.9 found: UploadStep.tsx line 112-117 has a useEffect with [isAuthenticated] as the dependency array, but the effect body reads uploadStarted from component state. The eslint-disable-next-line comment suppresses the exhaustive-deps warning. The uploadRef.current guard prevents actual double-execution in most cases, but the pattern is fragile: if isAuthenticated transitions false->true->false->true (e.g. session refresh), the effect fires again and uploadRef.current is already true so it silently does nothing — but the guard is a ref, not state, so React cannot track it. The correct fix is to add uploadStarted to the dependency array (the effect already checks !uploadStarted) and remove the eslint-disable comment, or restructure to use a 'Start Upload' button instead of auto-start.
close_reason: 4a1c1b6 fix: add uploadStarted and runUpload to useEffect deps, wrap runUpload in useCallback

title: Fix: publish-to-gbif.ts dry-run duplicates orchestrator logic instead of delegating (from green_globe-56.8)
description: Review of green_globe-56.8 found: The --dry-run path in scripts/publish-to-gbif.ts (lines 302-391) re-implements the full generate/upload/validate pipeline inline instead of delegating to publishToGbif(). The spec says 'delegate entirely to publishToGbif from publisher.ts'. This means: (1) buildZip is copy-pasted a third time (also in publisher.ts and export-dwca.ts), (2) fetchDwcaRecords + assembleDwca + uploadAndGetUrl + submitUrlForValidation + pollValidationUntilComplete are all called directly in the CLI, (3) any future bug fix to the generate/upload/validate steps in publisher.ts will NOT be reflected in the dry-run path. The fix is to add a dryRun option to PublishOptions (or a separate flag) so publishToGbif can stop before the register/crawl steps, and the CLI delegates to it. Evidence: scripts/publish-to-gbif.ts lines 43, 150-245, 302-391 vs src/lib/gbif/publisher.ts lines 6, 84-182.
close_reason: ec6c472 fix: delegate dry-run to publishToGbif, add dryRun option to PublishOptions

title: Fix: CLI passes bare DID as organizationAtUri instead of a valid organization record AT-URI (from green_globe-56.8)
description: Review of green_globe-56.8 found: scripts/publish-to-gbif.ts line 399 constructs organizationAtUri as `at://${args.did}` — a bare DID-rooted AT-URI with no collection or rkey. The lexicon (lexicons/app/gainforest/gbif/dataset.json) declares organizationRef with format: at-uri and the spec says it should be the 'AT-URI of the organization info record'. A bare `at://did:plc:...` is not a record reference — it should be something like `at://did:plc:.../app.gainforest.organization.info/self`. Consequence: the PDS record stores a non-record AT-URI, and the publisher's deduplication check (publisher.ts line 333: r.organizationRef === organizationAtUri) will fail to match on re-publish if the correct AT-URI is ever used. Evidence: scripts/publish-to-gbif.ts:399, src/lib/gbif/publisher.ts:54 (PublishOptions.organizationAtUri), lexicons/app/gainforest/gbif/dataset.json:16.
close_reason: 0da4999 fix: use full organization info AT-URI in GBIF publish CLI

title: Fix: GBIF_ORG_KEY missing-env check is dead code — orgKey always has a default (from green_globe-56.7, green_globe-56.8)
description: Review of green_globe-56.7 and green_globe-56.8 found: gbifConfig.orgKey in src/config/gbif.ts always has a value because of the ?? fallback to the hardcoded GainForest UUID ('c02486e8-eb54-4e94-81d8-1038cc58e208'). Therefore the check `if (!gbifConfig.orgKey) missing.push('GBIF_ORG_KEY')` in both scripts/bootstrap-gbif-installation.ts:68 and scripts/publish-to-gbif.ts:135 will never trigger — GBIF_ORG_KEY is silently ignored if unset. The non-null assertion gbifConfig.orgKey! on bootstrap line 106 is also unnecessary. This means operators cannot override the org key via env var and get a clear error if they forget — the default silently takes over. Fix: either remove the env check (document the default is intentional) or make orgKey required (no default) and always validate it. Evidence: src/config/gbif.ts:7-9, scripts/bootstrap-gbif-installation.ts:68,106, scripts/publish-to-gbif.ts:135.
close_reason: 03a8160 fix: remove hardcoded GBIF_ORG_KEY default, make env check live

title: Fix: buildZip is copy-pasted three times — extract to shared utility (from green_globe-56.6, green_globe-56.8)
description: Review of green_globe-56.6 and green_globe-56.8 found: The buildZip function (ZIP archive builder using node:zlib deflateRawSync + crc32) is copy-pasted identically in three places: (1) src/lib/gbif/publisher.ts lines 84-182 (comment says 'Mirrors the buildZip helper in scripts/export-dwca.ts'), (2) scripts/publish-to-gbif.ts lines 150-245 (comment says 'Mirrors the buildZip helper in publisher.ts'), (3) scripts/export-dwca.ts lines 89-177. Any bug fix or improvement to the ZIP builder must be applied in three places. The spec for green_globe-56.6 says publisher.ts should not implement publishing logic beyond orchestration. Fix: extract buildZip to src/lib/gbif/dwca/zip-builder.ts (or similar) and import it in all three files. Evidence: grep for buildZip across the three files.
close_reason: 0ccfa59 refactor: extract buildZip to shared zip-builder utility

title: Fix: findByGbifDatasetKey is exported but never imported — publisher uses inline filter instead (from green_globe-56.5, green_globe-56.6)
description: Review of green_globe-56.5 and green_globe-56.6 found: pds-dataset-registry.ts exports findByGbifDatasetKey (line 241) but it is never imported anywhere. The publisher.ts (green_globe-56.6) was supposed to use it per the spec ('Check if dataset already exists: call findByGbifDatasetKey from pds-dataset-registry.ts using listGbifDatasetRecords') but instead calls listGbifDatasetRecords directly and filters inline by organizationRef (publisher.ts:331-334). The two approaches are not equivalent: findByGbifDatasetKey filters by gbifDatasetKey (the GBIF UUID), while the publisher filters by organizationRef (the AT-URI). This means: (1) if a dataset was registered but the PDS record's organizationRef doesn't match the current organizationAtUri, the publisher will create a duplicate GBIF dataset instead of updating the existing one; (2) findByGbifDatasetKey is dead code. Fix: align the publisher's lookup strategy with the spec, or update the spec/function to match the actual intent. Evidence: src/lib/gbif/pds-dataset-registry.ts:241, src/lib/gbif/publisher.ts:331-334.
close_reason: 9a2142b fix: align publisher lookup with findByOrganizationRef, eliminate dead code

title: Fix: OAuth redirect after sign-in loses upload wizard state
description:
## Files
- src/app/_components/actions/oauth.ts (modify)
- src/app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts (modify)
- src/app/_components/dialogs/SignInBluesky/index.tsx (modify)
- src/app/upload/_components/UploadStep.tsx (modify)
- src/app/upload/_components/UploadWizard.tsx (modify)

## Root cause

The OAuth callback route (src/app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts line 54) hard-codes redirect('/') after successful auth. When the user signs in from /upload Step 4, they land on the home page and lose all wizard state.

## What to do — TWO parts

### Part 1: Fix the redirect URL (cookie-based return URL)

1. In src/app/_components/actions/oauth.ts, add an optional returnTo parameter to the authorize function:
   - async function authorize(handle: string, returnTo?: string): Promise<{ authorizationUrl: string }>
   - If returnTo is provided AND it starts with '/' (relative path only — security), set a cookie named 'oauth_return_to' with value=returnTo, path=/, maxAge=600 (10 min), httpOnly=true, sameSite='lax'
   - Use cookies() from 'next/headers' to set the cookie

2. In src/app/_components/dialogs/SignInBluesky/index.tsx:
   - Add an optional returnTo prop to the Props type: returnTo?: string
   - Pass returnTo to authorize(): authorize(handle.trim(), returnTo)

3. In src/app/api/oauth/callback/route.ts:
   - At the top of GET(), read the oauth_return_to cookie via cookies() from 'next/headers'
   - Delete the cookie immediately (set maxAge=0)
   - On line 54, replace redirect('/') with redirect(returnUrl || '/')
   - On line 56 (error case), keep redirect('/?error=auth_failed') — don't redirect errors to the return URL
   - SECURITY: validate returnUrl starts with '/' (no open redirect to external sites). If it doesn't start with '/', fall back to '/'

### Part 2: Persist wizard state across the OAuth redirect (sessionStorage)

4. In src/app/upload/_components/UploadStep.tsx:
   - Define a constant STORAGE_KEY = 'upload-wizard-pending'
   - When the component renders in the NOT authenticated state (the sign-in prompt branch, line 128-156), store the validRows to sessionStorage:
     useEffect(() => { if (!isAuthenticated && validRows.length > 0) { sessionStorage.setItem(STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify({ validRows, timestamp: Date.now() })); } }, [isAuthenticated, validRows]);
   - Pass returnTo='/upload' to the SignInBlueskyDialog component:
     <SignInBlueskyDialog returnTo='/upload' trigger={<Button>Sign in with ClimateAI</Button>} />
   - Export the STORAGE_KEY constant so UploadWizard can import it

5. In src/app/upload/_components/UploadWizard.tsx:
   - Import STORAGE_KEY from UploadStep
   - Add a useEffect that runs on mount to check for persisted state:
     useEffect(() => { const stored = sessionStorage.getItem(STORAGE_KEY); if (stored) { sessionStorage.removeItem(STORAGE_KEY); try { const { validRows, timestamp } = JSON.parse(stored); if (Date.now() - timestamp < 10 * 60 * 1000 && Array.isArray(validRows) && validRows.length > 0) { setState(prev => ({ ...prev, currentStep: 4, validRows })); } } catch { /* ignore corrupt data */ } } }, []);

## Don't
- Don't use query parameters or hash fragments for the return URL (cookie is safer)
- Don't allow returnTo to be an absolute URL or external URL — must start with '/'
- Don't modify the error redirect case (line 56) — errors always go to /?error=auth_failed
- Don't break the existing sign-in flow from the home page or other pages (returnTo is optional, defaults to '/')
- Don't change the OAuth token exchange or session saving logic in the callback
- Don't use localStorage — use sessionStorage (auto-clears when tab closes)
acceptance_criteria: 1. User on /upload Step 4 clicks 'Sign in with ClimateAI', completes OAuth, and returns to /upload (not /). 2. After returning, the wizard resumes at Step 4 with the valid rows preserved — upload auto-starts if authenticated. 3. Signing in from the home page (AccountButton) still redirects to / after auth. 4. The oauth_return_to cookie is deleted after being read in the callback. 5. If someone manually sets oauth_return_to to an absolute URL like 'https://evil.com', the callback falls back to '/'. 6. Persisted state in sessionStorage expires after 10 minutes. 7. bun run lint passes. 8. bun run build succeeds.
close_reason: 583770e fix: OAuth redirect after sign-in loses upload wizard state

title: Fix: info.json email field spec says format:uri which is semantically incorrect for email addresses (from green_globe-1.1)
description: Review of green_globe-1.1 spec found a semantic error in the task description itself: the spec says email should have 'format: uri' (string, format: uri, maxGraphemes: 256). Email addresses are NOT URIs — they are not valid URI format values in ATProto's lexicon system. The correct approach is either: (a) use type: string with maxGraphemes: 256 and no format (email is just a string), or (b) use format: 'email' if ATProto supports it. Using format: uri would cause validation failures for any email address that doesn't start with a URI scheme. This is a spec defect that needs to be corrected when task 1.1 is re-implemented. Note: this issue is secondary to green_globe-2 (the fields were never added at all), but the spec itself needs fixing before re-implementation.

title: Fix: GBIF Validator submitUrlForValidation sends wrong Content-Type (415 error)
description:
## Files
- src/lib/gbif/api/validator-client.ts (modify)

## What to do

The GBIF Validator OpenAPI spec (tmp/techdocs.gbif.org/openapi/validator.json) shows that POST /validation/url requires Content-Type: multipart/form-data. Our submitUrlForValidation function sends a POST with no body and no Content-Type header, causing HTTP 415 Unsupported Media Type.

Fix submitUrlForValidation() (line 50-52): pass an empty FormData as the body. When fetch receives a FormData body, it automatically sets Content-Type: multipart/form-data with the correct boundary.

Change from:
  const response = await fetch(url.toString(), { method: 'POST' })

To:
  const response = await fetch(url.toString(), { method: 'POST', body: new FormData() })

That is the entire fix — one line change.

## Don't
- Don't change the fileUrl query parameter approach (that's correct per spec)
- Don't change any other function in the file
- Don't change the polling or result checking logic
acceptance_criteria: 1. submitUrlForValidation sends Content-Type: multipart/form-data (via empty FormData body). 2. bun run lint passes. 3. bunx vitest passes.
close_reason: 2ee4615 fix: pass empty FormData body in submitUrlForValidation

title: Fix: createDataset returns quoted UUID causing 'UUID string too large' error
description:
## Files
- src/lib/gbif/api/registry-client.ts (modify)

## What to do

createDataset() on line 119 uses response.text() to parse the GBIF API response. GBIF returns the dataset UUID as a JSON-encoded string: '"f4dc06b3-..."' (with double quotes). response.text() returns this raw text INCLUDING the quotes (38 chars). This quoted string is then used in addContact, addEndpoint etc as the datasetKey in URL paths like /dataset/{key}/contact. GBIF rejects the 38-char quoted string with 400 'UUID string too large'.

Fix: change line 119 from:
  return response.text()
To:
  return response.json() as Promise<string>

This matches createInstallation() on line 92 which already correctly uses response.json().

The comment on line 118 ('GBIF returns a plain UUID string, not a JSON object') is misleading — GBIF returns a JSON-encoded string. Update the comment to: 'GBIF returns a JSON-encoded UUID string'.

## Don't
- Don't change any other function
- Don't change the response parsing for any other endpoint
acceptance_criteria: 1. createDataset returns a clean 36-char UUID without quotes. 2. bun run lint passes. 3. bunx vitest passes.
close_reason: 44d0577 fix: createDataset uses response.json() to strip quoted UUID

title: Fix: DwC-A archive blob gets garbage-collected — store as proper BlobRef to prevent PDS GC
description:
## Files
- lexicons/app/gainforest/gbif/dataset.json (modify)
- src/lib/gbif/pds-archive-host.ts (modify)
- src/lib/gbif/pds-dataset-registry.ts (modify)
- src/lib/gbif/publisher.ts (modify)

## Root cause

The DwC-A archive ZIP uploaded via com.atproto.repo.uploadBlob is stored in the app.gainforest.gbif.dataset record as a plain string CID (archiveBlobCid field). In ATProto, blobs are garbage-collected unless they are referenced by a proper blob object in at least one record. A plain string CID does NOT count as a blob reference. The PDS therefore GCs the blob, and GBIF's crawler gets HTTP 500 when trying to download the archive.

## What to do

### 1. Update the lexicon (lexicons/app/gainforest/gbif/dataset.json)

Add a new property archiveBlob with type blob, alongside the existing archiveBlobCid string. Both should be optional. The blob ref is what keeps the blob alive; the CID string is convenient for URL building.

Add to properties (after archiveBlobCid):
  "archiveBlob": {
    "type": "blob",
    "accept": ["application/zip"],
    "description": "Blob reference to the DwC-A archive ZIP. Storing as a proper blob ref prevents PDS garbage collection."
  }

### 2. Update pds-archive-host.ts

Change uploadDwcaBlob to ALSO return the raw blob object from the response, not just the CID string. The blob object is response.data.blob from agent.com.atproto.repo.uploadBlob. Return it alongside cid and mimeType.

Change the return type to: { cid: string; mimeType: string; blob: unknown }
(Use unknown since the blob object's exact type varies by SDK version — the caller passes it directly into the record.)

Change the return statement to:
  return { cid: blob.ref.toString(), mimeType: blob.mimeType, blob: response.data.blob }

Similarly update uploadAndGetUrl to return the blob object: { cid: string; url: string; blob: unknown }

### 3. Update pds-dataset-registry.ts

Add archiveBlob (unknown type, optional) to GbifDatasetRecordInput and GbifDatasetRecord types.

In createGbifDatasetRecord and updateGbifDatasetRecord, include archiveBlob in the record if provided:
  ...(data.archiveBlob !== undefined && { archiveBlob: data.archiveBlob })

### 4. Update publisher.ts

Where uploadAndGetUrl is called, capture the blob object:
  const { cid, url, blob } = await uploadAndGetUrl(agent, did, archiveBuffer)

Pass archiveBlob to createGbifDatasetRecord and updateGbifDatasetRecord:
  archiveBlob: blob

Also add archiveBlob to the existing archiveBlobCid field in the record data.

## Don't
- Don't remove the archiveBlobCid string field — keep it for backward compat and easy URL building
- Don't change the buildBlobUrl function — it still uses the CID string
- Don't change the lexicon-api codegen (it runs separately with bun run codegen:lexicon-api)
- Don't modify the GBIF registry client or validator client
acceptance_criteria: 1. After publishing, curl the blob URL and get HTTP 200 (not 500). 2. The app.gainforest.gbif.dataset record contains both archiveBlobCid (string) and archiveBlob (blob ref). 3. Re-running the publish script with --skip-validation against the same DID updates the existing dataset (not creates a duplicate). 4. bun run lint passes. 5. bunx vitest passes.
close_reason: ae6f2b0 fix: store DwC-A archive as proper BlobRef to prevent PDS GC

title: Epic: Adopt bundled dwc.measurement schema (flora/fauna unions)
description:
## Goal
Migrate from the old one-record-per-measurement dwc.measurement schema to the new bundled schema where all measurements for an occurrence are stored in a single record with a typed union (floraMeasurement / faunaMeasurement / genericMeasurement).

## Context
The measurement lexicon was redesigned in commit 696cade. The old schema required N PDS records for N measurements per occurrence (e.g., 4 records for height + DBH + diameter + canopyCover on a single tree). The new schema bundles all into 1 record, reducing PDS storage by ~60%.

## Scope
1. Regenerate lexicon API types from the updated lexicon
2. Update write path (pds-writer.ts, schemas.ts, types.ts, actions.ts) to produce bundle format
3. Update read path (use-organization-measured-trees.ts) to handle BOTH old + new formats
4. Update DwC-A export pipeline (pds-fetcher.ts, measurement-writer.ts) to handle both formats
5. Write a backfill migration script that converts old per-measurement records to bundles
6. Update all tests to match new format

## Constraints
- Both old and new format records will coexist on the PDS until migration completes
- All read paths MUST support both formats (backward compat)
- DwC-A export to GBIF must produce identical output regardless of storage format
- The upload UI (/upload) currently only handles tree data (flora), not fauna
- Migration scripts follow existing patterns in scripts/migrations/ (Node.js, resumable, dry-run support)
close_reason: ced5ef9 All 7 subtasks complete — write path, read path, DwC-A pipeline, and backfill script all updated

title: Regenerate lexicon-api types from updated measurement lexicon
description:
## Files
- lexicon-api/ (regenerate — DO NOT edit manually)

## What to do
Run `bun run codegen:lexicon-api` to regenerate the TypeScript types from the updated `lexicons/app/gainforest/dwc/measurement.json`. The new lexicon has 5 defs: main, floraMeasurement, faunaMeasurement, genericMeasurement, measurementEntry.

After codegen, verify the generated file at `lexicon-api/types/app/gainforest/dwc/measurement.ts` contains:
- A `Record` interface with `occurrenceRef`, `result` (union), and `createdAt` as required fields
- Exported interfaces for `FloraMeasurement`, `FaunaMeasurement`, `GenericMeasurement`, `MeasurementEntry`

## Don't
- Edit any file in lexicon-api/ manually
- Change any lexicon JSON files
- Modify any application code in this task
acceptance_criteria:
1. `bun run codegen:lexicon-api` exits 0
2. `lexicon-api/types/app/gainforest/dwc/measurement.ts` exports Record type with required fields: occurrenceRef, result, createdAt
3. `lexicon-api/types/app/gainforest/dwc/measurement.ts` exports FloraMeasurement, FaunaMeasurement, GenericMeasurement, MeasurementEntry interfaces
4. `bun run lint` passes (no new errors from generated code)
close_reason: 5a20ab5 Regenerate lexicon-api types from updated measurement lexicon

title: Update upload types and schemas to produce flora measurement bundles
description:
## Files
- src/lib/upload/types.ts (modify)
- src/lib/upload/schemas.ts (modify)
- src/lib/upload/schemas.test.ts (modify)

## What to do

### types.ts
Replace the `MeasurementInput` type with a new `FloraMeasurementBundle` type that matches the new lexicon shape:

```ts
export type FloraMeasurementBundle = {
  dbh?: string;          // value in cm
  totalHeight?: string;  // value in m
  diameter?: string;     // value in cm (maps to basalDiameter if non-DBH)
  canopyCoverPercent?: string; // value as percentage string
};
```

Update `ValidatedRow` to use:
```ts
export type ValidatedRow = {
  index: number;
  occurrence: OccurrenceInput;
  floraMeasurement: FloraMeasurementBundle | null; // null when no measurements present
};
```

Keep exporting a `MeasurementInput` type alias pointing to the old shape for backward compat with the DwC-A pipeline (add a `/** @deprecated */` JSDoc).

### schemas.ts
Change `extractMeasurements()` to `extractFloraMeasurement()`:
- Instead of building an array of {type, value, unit} objects, build a single `FloraMeasurementBundle` object
- Map: `height` → `totalHeight` (String), `dbh` → `dbh` (String), `diameter` → `diameter` (String), `canopyCover` → `canopyCoverPercent` (String)
- Return `null` if no measurement fields are present
- Update `parseAndValidateRows` to call this new function and populate `floraMeasurement` instead of `measurements`

### schemas.test.ts
Update all test assertions:
- Tests checking `row.measurements` array → check `row.floraMeasurement` object
- The test 'creates correct MeasurementInput objects for all measurement types' → becomes 'creates correct FloraMeasurementBundle from parsed fields', asserting `floraMeasurement.dbh === '25'`, `floraMeasurement.totalHeight === '10'`, etc.
- The test 'skips empty measurement fields' → assert `floraMeasurement` is `null`

## Don't
- Change column-mapper.ts or kobo-mapper.ts (those map CSV headers to field names, not to PDS records — they stay the same)

title: Update pds-writer to write bundled measurement records
description:
## Files
- src/lib/upload/pds-writer.ts (modify)
- src/app/upload/_components/actions.ts (modify)

## What to do

### pds-writer.ts
Replace `writeMeasurementsToPds` with `writeMeasurementBundleToPds`:

```ts
export async function writeMeasurementBundleToPds(
  agent: Agent,
  did: string,
  occurrenceUri: string,
  floraMeasurement: FloraMeasurementBundle
): Promise<{ uri: string }>
```

This function writes ONE record to the PDS collection `app.gainforest.dwc.measurement` with this shape:
```ts
{
  occurrenceRef: occurrenceUri,
  result: {
    $type: 'app.gainforest.dwc.measurement#floraMeasurement',
    dbh: floraMeasurement.dbh,            // if present
    totalHeight: floraMeasurement.totalHeight,    // if present  
    // ... only include non-undefined fields
    // Map diameter → basalDiameter field name in the lexicon
    canopyCoverPercent: floraMeasurement.canopyCoverPercent, // if present
  },
  createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
}
```

Key details:
- The `result` object MUST include `$type: 'app.gainforest.dwc.measurement#floraMeasurement'` — this is how ATProto union discrimination works
- Only include fields in result that have defined, non-empty values
- Use `TID.nextStr()` for rkey (same as current pattern)
- Use `agent.com.atproto.repo.putRecord` (same as current pattern)

Update `writeTreeRecord` to call the new function:
```ts
export async function writeTreeRecord(
  agent: Agent,
  did: string,
  row: { occurrence: OccurrenceInput; floraMeasurement: FloraMeasurementBundle | null },
  projectRef?: string
): Promise<{ occurrenceUri: string; measurementUri: string | null }>
```
- If `floraMeasurement` is null, skip the measurement write (return `measurementUri: null`)
- Return a single `measurementUri` string instead of array (since it's one record now)

Keep the old `writeMeasurementsToPds` function but mark it `/** @deprecated */` — don't delete it yet.

### actions.ts
Update `writeTreeRecordAction`:
- Change parameter type from `{ occurrence: OccurrenceInput; measurements: MeasurementInput[] }` to `{ occurrence: OccurrenceInput; floraMeasurement: FloraMeasurementBundle | null }`
- Change return type: `measurementUris: string[]` → `measurementUri: string | null`
- Update the call to `writeTreeRecord` accordingly

## Don't
- Delete the old `writeMeasurementsToPds` function (mark deprecated, keep for migration script use)
- Change the upload UI component (UploadStep.tsx) — it will need a separate small update to pass the new shape
acceptance_criteria:
1. `writeMeasurementBundleToPds` writes exactly 1 PDS record with `result.$type === 'app.gainforest.dwc.measurement#floraMeasurement'`
2. `writeTreeRecord` accepts `floraMeasurement: FloraMeasurementBundle | null` and returns `measurementUri: string | null`
3. `writeTreeRecordAction` server action has updated type signature
4. Old `writeMeasurementsToPds` still exists (marked deprecated)
5. `bun run lint` passes

title: Update measured trees read hook to handle both old and new measurement formats
description:
## Files
- src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_hooks/use-organization-measured-trees.ts (modify)

## What to do
Update `fetchMeasurementIndex` to handle BOTH the old per-measurement format AND the new bundled format.

### Detection logic
Each raw record from the PDS collection `app.gainforest.dwc.measurement` will be one of:

**Old format** (has `measurementType` + `measurementValue` at top level):
```json
{ "occurrenceRef": "at://...", "measurementType": "DBH", "measurementValue": "45.2", ... }
```

**New format** (has `result` object with `$type`):
```json
{ "occurrenceRef": "at://...", "result": { "$type": "app.gainforest.dwc.measurement#floraMeasurement", "dbh": "45.2", "totalHeight": "18.5", ... }, ... }
```

### Implementation
In `fetchMeasurementIndex`, after fetching each record:

1. Check if `v.result` exists and is an object — if so, it's new format:
   - Extract `v.result.dbh` → dbh
   - Extract `v.result.totalHeight` → height
   - Use `v.occurrenceRef` as the key
2. Else check if `v.measurementType` exists — if so, it's old format:
   - Existing logic: match on `measurementType.toLowerCase()` being 'dbh' or 'height'/'tree height'

The output type `MeasurementsByOccurrence` stays the same: `Map<string, { dbh?: string; height?: string }>`. Both code paths write into the same map.

### Type update
Update `RawMeasurementValue` to include the new fields:
```ts
type RawMeasurementValue = {
  occurrenceRef?: unknown;
  measurementType?: unknown;   // old format
  measurementValue?: unknown;  // old format
  measurementUnit?: unknown;   // old format
  result?: unknown;            // new format — will be object with $type
  [k: string]: unknown;
};
```

## Don't
- Change the occurrence fetching logic
- Change the multimedia fetching logic
- Change the GeoJSON feature construction
- Modify the hook's public API or return type
acceptance_criteria:
1. Hook correctly reads old-format measurement records (measurementType/measurementValue at top level)
2. Hook correctly reads new-format measurement records (result.dbh, result.totalHeight in floraMeasurement)
3. When an org has a mix of old and new records, both are read and indexed correctly
4. `bun run lint` passes
5. `bun run build` succeeds
close_reason: 1b08727 Update measured trees hook to handle both old and new measurement formats

title: Update DwC-A export pipeline to handle both measurement formats
description:
## Files
- src/lib/gbif/dwca/pds-fetcher.ts (modify)
- src/lib/gbif/dwca/measurement-writer.ts (modify)
- src/lib/gbif/dwca/__tests__/measurement-writer.test.ts (modify)

## What to do

### pds-fetcher.ts
Update the measurement mapping section (step 5, line ~250) to detect and flatten both formats.

For each raw measurement record:
1. If `v.result` exists and is an object → **new format**: flatten the typed fields into `PdsMeasurementRecord[]` rows:
   - Extract `v.occurrenceRef` as the common `occurrenceRef` for all generated rows
   - Extract `v.measuredBy` → `measurementDeterminedBy`, `v.measurementDate` → `measurementDeterminedDate`, `v.measurementMethod` → `measurementMethod`
   - For floraMeasurement (`v.result.$type` ends with '#floraMeasurement'):
     - Map each non-null typed field to a `PdsMeasurementRecord`:
       `dbh` → `{measurementType: 'DBH', measurementValue: v.result.dbh, measurementUnit: 'cm'}`
       `totalHeight` → `{measurementType: 'tree height', measurementValue: v.result.totalHeight, measurementUnit: 'm'}`
       `canopyCoverPercent` → `{measurementType: 'canopy cover', measurementValue: v.result.canopyCoverPercent, measurementUnit: '%'}`
       `basalDiameter` → `{measurementType: 'diameter', measurementValue: v.result.basalDiameter, measurementUnit: 'cm'}`
       (and any other populated flora fields — use a FLORA_FIELD_MAP constant for the mapping)
     - Also flatten `v.result.additionalMeasurements` array if present (each entry is already in type/value/unit shape)
   - For faunaMeasurement and genericMeasurement: similar approach (can be stubbed with a TODO for now since no fauna data exists yet)
2. Else → **old format**: existing mapping logic (direct passthrough to `PdsMeasurementRecord`)

The output `measurements: PdsMeasurementRecord[]` stays the same type — downstream `writeMeasurementTsv` doesn't need to change.

### measurement-writer.ts
No changes needed to the writer itself — it already consumes `PdsMeasurementRecord[]`. But update the `PdsMeasurementRecord` type to be imported from `pds-fetcher.ts` (it's currently duplicated in both files). Use a re-export.

### measurement-writer.test.ts
Add a new test case for bundled measurement records:
```ts
it('handles new-format bundled measurement records (flattened by fetcher)', () => {
  // The fetcher flattens bundles into PdsMeasurementRecord[] before they reach the writer,
  // so this test verifies the flattened output is correctly written to TSV
  const records = [
    { occurrenceRef: uri, measurementType: 'DBH', measurementValue: '45.2', measurementUnit: 'cm' },
    { occurrenceRef: uri, measurementType: 'tree height', measurementValue: '18.5', measurementUnit: 'm' },
  ];
  // ... standard assertion that TSV rows are correct
});
```

## Don't
- Change the occurrence or multimedia mapping logic in pds-fetcher.ts
- Change the DwC-A TSV column definitions
- Change the meta.xml or EML generation
acceptance_criteria:
1. `fetchDwcaRecords` correctly flattens new-format bundled measurement records into `PdsMeasurementRecord[]`
2. `fetchDwcaRecords` still correctly handles old-format per-measurement records
3. DwC-A export produces identical TSV output whether records are in old or new format (same measurementType, measurementValue, measurementUnit values)
4. `bunx vitest src/lib/gbif/dwca/__tests__/measurement-writer.test.ts` — all tests pass
5. `bun run lint` passes
close_reason: 23bc648 Update DwC-A export pipeline to handle both measurement formats

title: Write backfill migration script to bundle old measurement records
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/bundle-measurement-records.js (create)
- scripts/migrations/data/bundle-measurements-progress.json (create — auto by script)
- scripts/migrations/data/bundle-measurements-errors.json (create — auto by script)

## What to do
Write a Node.js migration script that:

1. **For each org** on the PDS:
   a. Fetch all records from `app.gainforest.dwc.measurement` collection
   b. Separate into old-format (has `measurementType` at top level) and new-format (has `result`)
   c. Skip orgs that have zero old-format records (already migrated or never had measurements)
   d. Group old-format records by `occurrenceRef` URI
   e. For each group: build a new bundled record:
      ```js
      {
        occurrenceRef: groupKey,
        result: {
          $type: 'app.gainforest.dwc.measurement#floraMeasurement',
          // Map old measurementType → new field name:
          // 'DBH' | 'dbh' → dbh
          // 'tree height' | 'height' | 'Height' → totalHeight
          // 'diameter' → diameter
          // 'canopy cover' → canopyCoverPercent
          // Anything else → goes into additionalMeasurements array
        },
        createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
      }
      ```
   f. Write the new bundled record via `putRecord` with a fresh TID rkey
   g. Delete the old individual records via `deleteRecord` for each rkey in the group
   h. Log progress

2. **Resumability**: Track progress in `scripts/migrations/data/bundle-measurements-progress.json` — store which orgs have been fully processed. On restart, skip already-done orgs.

3. **Error handling**: Log errors to `scripts/migrations/data/bundle-measurements-errors.json`. Continue to next org on failure (don't abort entire run).

4. **CLI flags**: Follow existing migration script patterns:
   - `--service <url>` — PDS endpoint (default: https://climateai.org)
   - `--credentials <path>` — JSON file with org credentials (`{handle, password}` entries)
   - `--handle <handle>` — Process single org only
   - `--dry-run` — Log what would happen without writing/deleting
   - `--limit <n>` — Process at most N orgs

5. **Safety**: Write the new bundle BEFORE deleting old records. If the write succeeds but deletes fail, the org will have duplicates (detectable) rather than data loss.

## Pattern reference
Follow the structure of `scripts/migrations/migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js`:
- Uses `@atproto/api` AtpAgent
- Uses `@atproto/common-web` TID for rkey generation
- Progress/error JSON tracking
- CLI argument parsing with --flags
- Requires authentication (`agent.login({identifier, password})`)

## Don't
- Use ESM imports — the migration scripts use CommonJS (`require()`)
- Modify any application source code
- Run the migration automatically — this is a manual CLI tool
acceptance_criteria:
1. Script runs with `node scripts/migrations/bundle-measurement-records.js --dry-run --handle <test-handle>` and prints a plan without writing
2. Script correctly groups old-format records by occurrenceRef
3. Script maps known measurementType strings to floraMeasurement fields (DBH→dbh, tree height→totalHeight, etc.)
4. Script puts unknown measurementType values into additionalMeasurements array
5. Script writes new bundle record BEFORE deleting old records (safe ordering)
6. Script resumes from progress file on restart (skips completed orgs)
7. Script logs errors per-org without aborting the entire run
close_reason: efd7c9b Write backfill migration script to bundle old measurement records

title: Epic: Filter globe to show only approved non-VCS organizations
description:
## Why
The green globe currently shows ALL organizations with visibility=Public from the PDS. We need to restrict it to only the 56 approved non-VCS organizations from the Airtable Organizations grid view. 20 VCS-* organizations must be excluded.

## Success looks like
- Globe shows exactly the 56 approved non-VCS organizations
- No VCS-* organizations appear on the globe or in search
- No organizations outside the approved list appear
- Performance improves (fewer PDS calls since we skip non-allowlisted repos early)

## Constraints
- Filter must be applied server-side in listAllOrganizations() for defense in depth
- DID-based allowlist (not name-based) for reliability
- The existing visibility=Public check must remain as a second layer of filtering
- No changes to API route shape or client components needed

title: Integrate approved-org allowlist filter into listAllOrganizations
description:
## Files
- src/lib/atproto/list-all-organizations.ts (modify)

## What to do
Import APPROVED_ORGANIZATION_DIDS from @/config/approved-organizations and add an early DID filter in listAllOrganizations() so only approved repos are processed.

### Exact changes

1. Add import at top of file:
   import { APPROVED_ORGANIZATION_DIDS } from '@/config/approved-organizations';

2. After the pagination do-while loop (after line 33, before the Promise.all on line 36), insert a filter step. Add a comment '// Step 1.5: Filter to only approved organization DIDs (allowlist from Airtable)' and then filter: const approvedRepos = repos.filter((repo) => APPROVED_ORGANIZATION_DIDS.has(repo.did));

3. On the Promise.all line (currently line 37), change repos.map to approvedRepos.map. Everything else inside the .map callback stays exactly the same.

### Behavior
- Only repos whose DID is in APPROVED_ORGANIZATION_DIDS proceed to the info-fetch step
- The existing visibility === 'Public' check on line 57 MUST remain (defense in depth)
- Non-approved repos are silently skipped before any getRecord calls (performance win)
- No changes to the function signature, return type, or options parameter

### Performance impact
- Before: ~197 getRecord calls (one per PDS repo) plus coordinate fetches
- After: ~56 getRecord calls (only approved repos) plus coordinate fetches
- Saves ~141 unnecessary PDS calls per request

## Don't
- Remove or weaken the existing visibility === 'Public' filter
- Change the function signature or return type
- Change getOrganizationCoordinates()
- Touch any other file (API route, hooks, components)
- Add any new exported functions or types
acceptance_criteria:
1. listAllOrganizations() only processes repos whose DID is in APPROVED_ORGANIZATION_DIDS
2. Import statement uses @/config/approved-organizations path alias
3. The existing visibility === 'Public' check is still present and unchanged
4. The function signature and return type are unchanged
5. Only src/lib/atproto/list-all-organizations.ts is modified (plus the new import)
6. bun run lint passes
7. bun run build passes (no type errors)

title: Fix: stale ~197 PDS calls comment in route.ts after allowlist reduces calls to ~56 (from green_globe-74)
description: Review of green_globe-74 found: The comment in src/app/api/list-organizations/route.ts (line 6) still reads 'instead of making ~197 PDS calls per request'. After the allowlist filter introduced in green_globe-74.2, only ~56 getRecord calls are made (one per approved org), not ~197. The comment is now misleading about the actual performance characteristics. Evidence: route.ts line 6 — '// so Next.js caches the response for 5 minutes instead of making ~197 PDS calls per request.' The epic constraint said not to touch the API route, so this was not updated. Fix: update the comment to reflect the new ~56 call count (or a range like ~56-112 accounting for coordinate fetches).

title: Fix: test 5 in approved-organizations.test.ts is tautological — Set deduplicates silently (from green_globe-74.3)
description: Review of green_globe-74.3 found: Test 5 ('contains no duplicate entries') reconstructs a Set from the Set's own entries and checks sizes match. Since JavaScript's Set constructor deduplicates automatically, this test can never fail — even if the source array literal in approved-organizations.ts contains a duplicate DID, the Set will silently collapse it to 55 entries and test 5 will still pass (both sides of the comparison will be 55). The real guard against accidental duplicates is test 1 (size === 56), which would catch a silent dedup. Evidence: src/config/__tests__/approved-organizations.test.ts lines 31-35. Fix: replace test 5 with a test that checks the raw source array for duplicates, e.g. by exporting the raw array alongside the Set, or by parsing the source file. Alternatively, document that test 1 is the real duplicate guard and remove test 5 as redundant.

title: Epic: Integrate Hyperindex GraphQL for DWC occurrence/measurement queries
description:
## Goal
Replace direct ATProto PDS listRecords calls for dwc.occurrence and dwc.measurement with Hyperindex GraphQL queries (https://api.hi.gainforest.app/graphql). This eliminates massive over-fetching where the entire occurrence collection is downloaded and ~90-97%% is discarded client-side.

## Context
- Hyperindex already indexes: app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence, app.gainforest.dwc.measurement, app.gainforest.dwc.event
- Hyperindex does NOT index: app.gainforest.ac.multimedia (image data still fetched from PDS)
- Hyperindex is read-only (no mutations). The GraphQL API supports typed collection queries with where filters, Relay-style cursor pagination (max 100/page), sorting, and WebSocket subscriptions.
- Endpoint: POST https://api.hi.gainforest.app/graphql (no auth required)
- The dynamicProperties field is a JSON string in Hyperindex (same as PDS) — client-side JSON.parse still needed

## Scope
5 tasks: hyperindex client module, adapter layer, then 3 file migrations (measured-trees hook, occurrences hook, predictions store utils). No new npm dependencies — plain fetch.

## Success Criteria
- All 5 DWC occurrence fetch sites use Hyperindex instead of PDS listRecords
- ac.multimedia fetching stays on PDS (not indexed by Hyperindex)
- Existing React Query keys unchanged — downstream components unaffected
- fetchMeasuredTreeOccurrences export signature and return type unchanged
- S3 fallback functions untouched
- bun run lint and bun run build pass

3. src/lib/hyperindex/queries.ts exists and exports MEASURED_TREE_OCCURRENCES_QUERY, MEASUREMENTS_BY_DID_QUERY, OCCURRENCES_BY_KINGDOM_QUERY, PLANT_PREDICTIONS_QUERY
4. All queries use typed collection fields (appGainforestDwcOccurrence, appGainforestDwcMeasurement) NOT the generic records query
5. All queries include pageInfo { hasNextPage endCursor } and accept $first/$after variables
6. queryAllPages correctly follows cursors until hasNextPage is false
